reviews.text:
I was T-boned by vehicle being driven at high speed. I had fractures in my neck and lower back. I was referred to Mr Bacetich by my Brother who has used Nic's services in the past. My Brother was extremely pleased with his services.
When I first met Mr Bacetich he put me right at ease. His staff is not only friendly but they were very supportive & they kept me updated how My case progressed. My MVA resulted in surgery of my neck and lower back which has healed up pretty darn well not perfect but I can function pretty good.
Nic is very supportive empathetic which people at ease is friendly I felt all the time he was in my corner he listens carefully he's got great integrity and I received a fair award for my pain and suffering I would recommend Nick to anyone who needs somebody in their corner.  Jon H Campbell

Four years ago, Nic Bacetich walked into my hospital room. My road to recovery was long. Dealing with a case & the drunk driver was the last on my mind. Nic has been God-sent. He dove right in. This case was harder than we anticipated. It took an immense amount of dedication, time, proof & knowledge. We met many times in person, over phone & over emails. He remained honest, patient & professional from start to finish. As time went on I was accumulating more & more medical bills. From surprise surgeries, to treatments, therapies & miscellaneous medical spendings. Without Nic I would have been stuck with hundreds of thousands of dollars to pay off. His assistant Lorrie has been tremendous help. Very attentive & responsive. They have great connections & go the extra mile. They truly, truly care about their clients & have become like family. Nic Bacetich has given us the results we’ve never thought were achievable. I’m forever & ever grateful. Without the help of Nic, I would not have had the successful outcome. It was a long road, but very well worth it.

When tragedy hits and it's virtually impossible to think - where do you turn?  If it quickly becomes glaringly apparent that a solid legal opinion and sound guidance is a necessity - who would you call?  In 2009 - that type of tragic circumstance hit with the overwhelming,  rapid decline of my father's health in a rehab facility - resulting in his death.  We were in shock.  Immediately, we turned to Dominic L. Bacetich Law.  That paramount decision proved to be the very best step for my family.  Why?  In our most distressing time period of grief Nic provided empathy, knowledge and rock-solid guidance.  YES - our Dad was gone, never to return - however, "the end result" to this legal challenge was resolved as successfully as it could be solely because of Nic and his amazing staff.  No question.  Wrongful death is not an easy topic and never anticipated, however we look back and KNOW that our father's life was honored and the mis-handling of his care and subsequent death were acknowledged and properly dealt with.  Many thanks to Nic and staff for their exemplary assistance in our time of great need.  We will always remain thankful.

Phil was awesome in helping me set up an LLC. These guys know their stuff and I am so glad I was referred to them!

Thank you Phil!
The brief free consult I had with intake was excellent and very supportive. I would recommend working with these folks based on my experience with them so far.
Excellent attorney.
Best experience I have had to date.
Excellent attorney
Todd Nichols is wonderful to work with and his staff is very efficient and quite helpful!
Amy was fantastic! Professional, thorough, explained things very well and was also very personable. Thank you!

reviews.text: Very professional, and very knowledgeable
Amy helped me with my auto accident. She was very professional and got me the best outcome that could have happened in this situation. I highly recommend her for auto/injury litigation.
Amy provided us excellent service and completed the work we needed in a timely manner.  She was very thorough, professional and patient with us.  I would highly recommend her to anyone.

Amy, we cannot thank you enough for all of the work you did for us.  Despite many delays - all on us, you maintained a patient and helpful demeanor.  Your assistance with providing witnesses for us was also greatly appreciated .  You are a credit to your profession!

Jerry and AJ Cooper

Amy was efficient, easy to work with and had great availability to suit our needs. Our materials were prepared with great care. We’d absolutely see her again and would recommend her to anyone looking for estate planning services.
Amy was the best.  Thorough, professional and great to work with.  Thank you!

I was involved in a hit-and-run accident that left me seeking questions on how to proceed with the situation. Long story short, I felt I had no closure to the accident as the police could not help, so I sought legal advice. Angie represented me with my claim and helped alleviate the many stressors that came following the accident. She facilitated the investigation process and was steadfast with her work.
If you have an MVA claim, I would consider Angie to represent you. She’s a hard worker and she advocates for her clients.

My mother was rear- ended on the freeway and hired Angie as our Lawyer. I’ve personally been in car accidents before and based on previous experience dealing with settlements in the past with other lawyers, my family was thankful we hired Angie this time. She was very caring, had great communication along the entire process, and we received more than expected in our settlement. The entire process was smooth and seamless and we were grateful to have had her as our lawyer. Trustworthy Lawyer.

Angie represented me after a car accident that left me with back problems and a chipped tooth.  I was brand new to the area and I was really struggling with the transition and some frustrating situations that happened, such as this accident.  Angie was so sweet and sincere, and I felt like she really cared about me and my situation.  She was honestly a God send in my dire time of need.  She was able to get me more than I anticipated and was so quick and responsive throughout the entire process.  I honestly did not feel like I needed to worry once I had met with her the first time, and sure enough my gut was right.  Angie consistently kept me informed with updates of my case and I would absolutely recommend her to anyone who needs a lawyer that feels like a friend.
Raymond was very helpful and kind.

I utilized the services of Raymond a couple of years ago as I needed help evicting my renters who were not paying me rent.
Raymond is a nice guy with a heart of gold who actually cares about his clients, has always shown empathy, and is a great communicator because that is what attorneys do.  He is also good at advising me when to shut up as he needs to advise and do his job without being interrupted every second. He is a true professional and didn't pass The BAR sucking  lollipops. He also knows his law thoroughly, and is fair as can be on his billing as well. I am proud to have known him and to still know him. Raymond Mutchler, attorney at law,(nice ring) PLLC whatever that means.
I learned what DUE PROCESS is from the judge yesterday, so I may be on my way as well.   MERRY CHRISTMAS, RAY.

Ray has been very helpful with the investment property I have. Since working with him on an early lease termination for my duplex, my husband and I have been far less stressed with making sure we handle things properly. We do not use a property management company and so we often need someone to talk to about our leases and tenants. Especially when they want to break their lease early. Which has now happened to us twice since starting to work with Raymond over a year ago. But luckily Raymond knows the laws and can help us as landlords handle the situation not just legally but also in a way that is fair for both parties.

Raymond himself is fair. You don’t feel nickel and dimed like some attorneys can make you feel. He really is a nice person to have in your corner whether you are a landlord or a tenant. I also appreciate the advice giving phone calls he has with my husband. Where we often both feel like we got better advice than we could have found on RHA or the internet.

Communication is also very good. We have retained other attorneys in the past and some were very reputable and competent. But not as communicative as Raymond. So that is a plus as well.

Raymond helped me with an issue with one of my tenants and I can't recommend him highly enough. His strategic guidance, quick thinking and ability to convince me to follow his recommended next steps took a situation that could have been a complete nightmare and turned it around in a matter of days. His fees were very reasonable and he was a really nice person to work with. Highly recommend him to any landlord needing someone they can trust to guide them through a sticky situation. Thank you, Raymond!

I received his number as a referral from Frank Volkert, of Real Property Management. I called Raymond, we spoke about my situation with my tenant, and he took my number and set us up to meet at his office today for a consultation. When I called the office today I found out he put my number in a "do not call database" to make it seem as if his number is disconnected. Waste of time.

I have nothing but good things to say about Mr Mutchler. Evictions is a messy business. He told me worst case scenarios,  that way I wouldn't get my hopes up. My case ended very well.  Following his advice had everything to do with it. Thank you Ray

I have found Ray to be not only pleasant to work with, but more genuine than other attorneys I have interviewed before. I have retained over 4 attorneys in the past, and I feel that my opinion of Mr. Mutchler holds some credibility. I currently have 2 other attorneys on retainer right now.

Raymond is not the type of attorney that makes you feel like he is "Nickle and Diming you". His method of billing for his services is reasonable. And within reason, he will be supportive and helpful with communication here and there and it won't be guaranteed to be on the invoice. But typical lawyer fees should be expected, his time is valuable.

However, for example, a friend of mine currently has an attorney that if you email him and ask a question regarding a letter that was already paid for, my friend will get billed for that question. Literally one question regarding a document that he paid for could not be asked without receiving an additional invoice.

Another example is one attorney I have had in the past was very poor with communication, but extremely competent in the court room. A very powerful attorney to have in your corner, but sometimes you have to send him more than one email with a question because he wouldn't always reply, ever, not just in a timely fashion. That is not Raymond, he is very attentive with communication.

Another attorney I had would repeat themselves a lot while billing me for every second of the conversation. It was very annoying because when you are paying an attorney by the minute for a verbal conversation, the last thing you want is for the conversation to go around in circles. He would also forget that he had already told me about something and repeat it to me. That is not Raymond. While he may have a short pause to contemplate something you asked him, he is generally efficient with getting his advice to you without running out the timer.

Raymond is also nice, I have had attorneys that are nice too, but try to rush you off the phone or have a tone in their voice like they don't like talking to you. Not Raymond. He gives off genuine vibes despite it being work for him, you feel like a friend is talking to you.

Knowledge wise, Raymond has taken the time to review a lease and find faults with it that was inherited by the acquisition of a duplex and it was drafted by a prominent, successful property management group. He identified that the lease was following outdated laws and verbiage with a buy-out clause that was not as clear as it should ideally be.

Raymond also gave me advice for a lease I needed for my rental property that at the end of the day, saved me money and avoided paying him a lot for it. That was something that he did which keeps me coming back to him for my investment properties. He is in it for the well being of his clients, not just the money.

Raymond Mutchler is knowledgeable, personable and reasonable. And he follows the concept that has long term benefits from taking care of his clients, instead of some professionals that focus on the short term benefits of getting as much money as possible from a client but will likely cause them to not be a repeat customer. Unlike one attorney I interviewed that offered to charge me $1,300 to file a LLC application that takes 30 minutes. Just filing, not writing!

Extreme limited knowledge of Real Estate. Incorrect fillings on Eviction or transactions. Rude, insulting, looks down on people when they correct him about legal real estate matters. I'd give a zero if that was an option. DO NOT HIRE. UNEDUCATED
Very mean didn't even let me talk

Mr. Mutchler was very welcoming and knowledgeable about Eviction law.
I never thought I would get such level of detail and understanding from a lawyer, especially in an area of law in which there are very little practicing law on behalf of landlords.
Very good.
Ray is very professional and experienced attorney. He handled our case carefully and gave us good advice along the process that saved us time, money and trouble.

I highly recommend Raymond Mutchler.. He is an honest straight forward attorney who is knowledgeable about how the court system works. Because of this I was able to avoid wasting time and money. I would use him again if I ever needed to.

I spoke with different attorneys and Ray stands out as being most knowledgeable with the most competitively priced service.  He took care of everything and he had my complete confidence throughout the process.  Ray has also provided legal guidance for the next time I consider asking a friend to move in.

Greg was able to assist me with a horrible landlord after falling when this landlord decided to make a tripping hazard after having all residents move the community trash to a common walk area causing a safety hazard.  Greg had a lot of confidence and was determined to help.  I happily endorse him as highly proficient attorney.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Review for Greg Marshall, Attorney at Law

Greg Marshall began representing me in 2019 for a medical malpractice case against the largest retail pharmaceutical chains in the U.S. He believed in my case from the beginning, when no one else would. I had no experience with personal injury law and would have settled early (and low) if Greg hadn’t taken the time to educate me about my rights and guide me through the process with patience and care.

Over five years of litigation, we faced countless continuances, pandemic delays, and mediation setbacks — all leading to an eight-week trial in King County Superior Court. It was one of the hardest experiences of my life, second only to the malpractice itself. Through every challenge, Greg was calm, patient, and unwaveringly supportive.

The jury unanimously found the nurse negligent and her employer responsible for permanent damages far beyond what I ever imagined. While no amount can undo the harm, the verdict gave my family and me a sense of validation and justice. It was also the first plaintiff verdict in Washington state for sepsis-associated encephalopathy — making it even more meaningful.

Greg practices law with the highest integrity, always making recommendations rooted in my best interests. His deep knowledge of medical and personal injury law, combined with his network of expert witnesses, gave my case a strong foundation. He has a rare ability to turn complex medical and legal details into a clear, compelling story.

Most importantly, Greg showed genuine compassion for me and my children. He explained everything in plain language, treated everyone with respect, and handled every moment — even the toughest ones — with professionalism and empathy.

I’ll always be grateful for Greg’s perseverance, integrity, and belief in my case. If you’re facing a difficult medical malpractice or personal injury matter, I can’t recommend him enough. Greg fights with both intelligence and heart — and that combination made all the difference.

Greg was such a vital support in getting one of the most stressful things of my life situated. I had been worked over with an insurance company that wouldn’t communicate with me to settle my auto claim and it was eating up my time and life and I was at my wits end. Enter Greg and he was able to get this settled quickly and got me a fair settlement and was so great at communicating with me on every step he was taking. He was willing to go to the mat with me on this and helped me feel so much better. He was very personable, kind, willing to explain all the little nuances and just overall made this a much more pleasant experience. I highly recommend him! Thanks Greg!

I got into a car accident a few years back and Gregory made the whole process extremly easy and made it stress free. I really can’t overstate how much he helped me with me with everything, was extremely patient and understanding the entire time. I was able to get treatment for a few a years and I don’t think the situation would’ve gone as well as it did if it wasn’t for him. He’s consistently great and I wouldn’t recommend anyone else but him!

I highly recommend Greg Marshall. He helped me recover damages related to my food poisoning case against a prominent restaurant. It happened at my daughter's wedding, no less. I found Greg to be knowledgeable, compassionate, and with a vast expertise in negotiating the best settlement. Thankfully, my case didn't involve court appearances. Greg handled obtaining all the medical records, correspondence, and negotiations. These cases are time consuming, but I am satisfied with the outcome. I am confident that he employs the same methodical and detail oriented approach in every case.

I highly recommend Robert O’Neal if you are looking for a criminal defense attorney!

He was professional, knowledgeable, and extremely responsive. He explained every step of the process clearly and made sure I understood my options. I felt respected and supported throughout a very stressful situation. Robert’s experience and dedication truly made a difference in my case!

Glen represented me on a vehicular assault case. In the years between the time the incident happened and the time they filed the case, I turned my life around, but the prosecutor wouldn’t give me a good plea deal, despite all the work I did in between the incident and them charging the case. He was able to collect statements and letters from my friends and family and put together a mitigation package and convinced the judge to give me a lenient sentence which allowed me to keep my job and move on with my life. I cannot put into words the amount of gratitude I have for Glen, and though I hope to not need his services in the future, he is the one and only person I would choose to represent me again.
SP
reviews.ownerResponse: Sonya, it was an absolute honor to work with you. Thank you for trusting me with your case. I'm so glad we were able to get a just outcome for you, and while I know you won't need my services again for a criminal case, please don't hesitate to reach out if there's anything I can do to help.

reviews.text:
I contacted Glen to help me understand the legal implications of a problem a family member of mine was having.  As soon as we began to discuss the issue I knew I had made the right decision.

Glen was extremely helpful.  His advice was clear and concise and his thoughtful questions helped quickly clarify how to proceed.

Glen assessed the issue and guided me to the forms that I needed to fill out and the process I needed to follow in order to rectify the situation.

Glen’s thorough approach and his competent yet pleasant manner made what I had worried would be a painful process much easier than I had anticipated.  I enjoyed working with him and would highly recommend him to anyone looking for legal help.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Michael, thanks so much for your kind words! It was a pleasure to work with you and I'm so happy we were able to assist. Please don't hesitate to reach out with any other legal needs.

reviews.text:
Glen was appointed to me and my case where I was falsely accused of multiple domestic violence crimes by my ex boyfriend; assault, malicious mischief and criminal trespassing. Even though I was actually the victim the courts put a pre-trial no contact order on me. While the case was pending Glen was able to get the pre-trial no contact lifted and helped me seek a no contact order against my ex. The case went to trial as the prosecutors were not willing to dismiss it. However Glen treated my case as if it were his own and was able to prove the lack of credibility and showed the transparency of the situation exposing lies and deception. The jury quickly came back with a verdict of not guilty on all counts. I truly felt safe and protected with his guidance and representation as he listened and took all my information and experiences into account. He was a huge influence in my breakthrough for moving forward and being able to live my life normally again and completely putting all of this behind me. Words don't even express my gratitude, Glen is all around amazing at what he does.
Taylor
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Taylor, thanks for taking the time to share this. It was an honor to represent you, and I appreciate you placing your trust in me. I wish you all the best in your future endeavors, and please don't hesitate to contact me if there's anything I can do to assist.

-Glen Rice

reviews.text:
Sharing on behalf of Jeremy Jackson:

After two weeks in jail, I was appointed Mr. Glen Rice, Esq, as my public defender. I was in a place of quiet despair until Glen came into my life.

Glen helped me get out of jail (he contacted my beloved aunt, Marla) and I was quickly in a new, safe environment. However, the circumstances that landed me in jail were complex due to the other party (a former girlfriend).  I was completely overwhelmed with what needed to be done to secure that all charges would be dropped. Glen helped me every step of the way, going above and beyond, in my humble opinion.

Glen worked tirelessly on my behalf. He was instrumental in proving that the other party was not mentally stable, therefore, other claims she made were tossed out.

Glen has helped me restart my life and I’m eternally grateful to him. I feel indebted to him, and I can’t recommend him highly enough.

Sincerely,

Jeremy Jackson
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Jeremy and Vanessa,

Thank you for taking the time to write about and share this. Jeremy was a great client to work for and I appreciate his trust in me. I'm pleased that, at the end, all charges against you were dismissed with prejudice. Best of luck to you in all things moving forward!

-Glen Rice

reviews.text: Tristen is very cold and not personable. She handled my first case, and there was little communication. I was served papers while she represented me and she said is not able to take on my case. I ended up going to Canfield Madow, and give Canfield Madow 5 stars for their service and attentiveness.

I had a frustrating experience with key law office  ( piper hackett) After reaching out via email for assistance with our case, I never received a response. I had high hopes for their service based on the reviews I read, but the lack of communication left me feeling ignored and unimportant . It’s vital to feel supported and informed, especially during challenging times. Unfortunately,  key law office did not meet my expectations and I would not recommend them based on my experience. I hope they can improve their client communication moving forward.
Tristan is an absolutely brilliant attorney, so glad I found her she’s relentless and I wouldn’t want anyone else in my corner!

Tristan came across very sympathetic and caring during our first meeting when I was deciding to hire her or not.  After that it all went down hill.  During my legal separation she made many careless mistakes filling out paperwork and when mistakes were pointed out she was rude and short tempered.  At one point she even sent me word documents and told me that I should make the corrections myself.  She charged for time that she used to make corrections and withdrew as my representative before the final paperwork was even delivered to me.  I had to hire another attorney to complete the work.  I would highly recommend that you look elsewhere for representation.

Staff was very rude, I asked about the intake process and they pressed me to schedule an appointment so they could charge me. I wanted to ask more questions and she told me to call someone else.
Sure, I will call someone else.

Tristen in my book is one of the most professional attorney’s around. My experience with her was amazing and she went above and beyond to help me. She puts her heart into her work and at times I was stressed she was there to keep me positive and focused. I highly recommend Tristen Key.

I'm a person with a disability, I needed an advocate. After getting my money  I was also dropped !my case was not completed and I'm still dealing with the mess she left for me without telling me it wasn't done.
I feel like I was taken advantage of by Tristan Keys law office in Everett Wa.

Unethical attorney. I was raised by two attorneys who both pro temp judge and they as well as attorney friends were shocked by some of the things I saw. Not to mention her legal assistant is horribly rude and has no compassion at all. Steer clear. Brewe Layman in Everett is amazing.

My personal experience with Key Law Offices was bad. I received conflicting/contradicting advice and information. When I asked for clarification, I was insulted and immediately dropped as a client, then literally yelled at by a staff member for asking if they had fixed an error they made in my case. They ultimately fixed the error, after I repeatedly insisted they do so.

Mrs. Cassandra helped me during the most stressful time I’ve ever gone through .  I don’t know if I would’ve gotten through without her assistance.  She cares about the well-being of all her clients and I 100% recommend her if you’re in need of legal assistance.
Good
Not recommended at all.
Cassandra is the top of her field, she is an excellent attorney and a very sweet person too.

Mrs Lopez is great at what she does. She knows how to handle each situation with the entire case like a pro. She knows the law, and won't stop until there's a fair outcome. She understands blur collar working people, so she relates and is completely fair about her fees. I've recommended friends and now recommending everyone.

Ms. Lopez is the greatest! I worked with her on several cases. She is very quick to respond, she knows the law, and she works hard to get the best outcome for each of her cases. I highly recommend her.

My experience with Mrs. Lopez was not a satisfactory one. I did not get the legal advise necesary to fight my case. The money given to Mrs. Lopez was lost with little or no assistance to resolve my case.

Derek has been very helpful explaining every step of the process, and as new things pop up. It was a professional experience and he is easy to get along with. All my questions were answered very quickly, and things were wrapped up faster than I expected. I would 100% recommend Derek to anyone in need of a lawyer.
reviews.ownerResponse: Joshua, thank you for trusting me to handle your case and for sharing your experience!

reviews.text:
My experience with Derek was nothing short of exceptional. When my car accident happened, I knew I needed help. I had never hired a lawyer before, and didn’t know where to start or what to expect. A close friend referred Derek to me, and I’m so glad they did!

Derek was patient, kind, understanding of my situation and what I’d lost, and listened to my concerns. Most importantly, he was informative. He walked me through every step of the process, explained things as many times as I needed, and also took my feedback when making decisions about my case. I truly felt like Derek fought for me every step of the way, for the best outcome possible.

I’m so grateful to have had his help through this experience, and would tell each person reading this that hiring Derek was the best decision I could have made! You should do the same.

Thank you for trusting me to handle your case.  You were a five star client.  Feel free to call anytime you have a question.

reviews.text:
I cannot recommend Derek Radtke highly enough! After my car accident, I was overwhelmed and unsure of where to turn, but from the moment I reached out to Derek, I felt like I was in the best hands possible. Derek was professional, knowledgeable, and compassionate every step of the way.

He took the time to explain the entire legal process to me, answered all my questions, and made sure I felt comfortable and informed throughout. The level of attention he gave to my case was outstanding, and he worked tirelessly to ensure I received the compensation I deserved.

Not only did he secure a great settlement, but he also made a challenging and stressful time much easier to navigate. If you're looking for an attorney who truly cares about their clients and delivers exceptional results, look no further than Derek Radtke. You won't be disappointed.

reviews.text: As a First Responder, I found myself in the back of my ambulance treating a patient en route to the hospital when a drunk driver collided with us. Though the impact wasn't severe, it was enough to jolt me and initiate chronic issues that will persist for life. Seeking legal representation, I turned to Derek Radtke. Without a moment's hesitation, I chose him to advocate on my behalf. Throughout the process, Derek's unwavering communication and reassurance provided me with solace. While I hope never to require his services again, if faced with a similar circumstance, he would undoubtedly be my first choice. I place my complete trust in him, and you should too. Many thanks for his outstanding support!
reviews.ownerResponse: Amber, it was a pleasure working with you, and thank you for choosing me to work with you on your case.

reviews.text: Great attorney works his butt off to get you a win. And a great person away from business. If I need an attorney again he will be my first call.

reviews.text: My daughter and I were in a serious car accident last year. I broke several bones, had surgery to place plates and pins, and was off work for almost 4 months. This was my first experience (fortunately) with an injury like this, and had no idea how to proceed. We were referred to Derek, and he made a horrible year bearable. He negotiated on our behalf, and made sure that we received the maximum benefit possible from insurance. He answered questions in a timely manner, and always made sure that we understood exactly what was going on. We couldn't have gotten through this without him. I highly recommend Derek Radtke.

Stop claims is efficient at what they do. They know how to manipulate and create road blocks in the LNI system that was intended to help injured workers pay their bills when they were hurt.

My son was injured on the job and his employer hired Stop Claims to "fight" a legitimate claim. In order to ultimately get my son the medical and financial support he needed, I had to help pay his rent for many months and help him hire an attorney to defend him. He lost all medical while his claim was appealed and couldn't get physical therapy he needed for more than five months. He also had to share his payroll compensation with the attorney to pay for the legal services he needed to respond to Stop Claims many actions.

The facts of his case were pretty clear and he quickly prevailed after an investigation and an appeal, but Stop Claims made sure he had no medical or financial support for almost six months while they used every legal means they could to delay payments and medical support.

While they are effective, they are morally and ethically corrupt as their business model is based on harming people who need help. While there are some people that are dishonest with LNI, most aren't and companies like Stop Claim make life harder when people need support most and they lack the expertise or compassion to discern legitimate claims from fraudulent ones. Their model is  all claims should be challenged and that is the approach they took with our family.

I don't recommend using Stop Claim any more than I would recommend going to a pawn shop for a loan or gambling in a casino to get rich. Ultimately, corporations like Stop Claim exist by hurting people, and from my perspective whoever owns and runs the company can only be described as an uncaring person at best or a sociopath at worst. I don't have much sympathy for companies whose survival is dependent on inflicting harm on people when they most need help.

If you are a narcissistic, greedy, callous business owner, this could be a good company to use, but if you have an ounce of compassion for your employees, please don’t subject them to the trauma and pain Stop Claim causes to achieve their results.

Bad experience – they ghosted me. I called Stop Claims Corp and spoke to Kayla for help with my matter. We discussed my appointment date and time, going back and forth. I completed the paperwork, sent it back to them. After that, I never heard from anyone. I called all their phone numbers and left voicemails, but no one called me back. I got to the point that it was too late to  get representation from other firms. I lost a lot of time with Stop Claims, which put me in a bad situation. and after my appointment Kayla called me back — I dont think that I would use them after what I had experienced. I don’t know why they all ghosted me. After this experience, I don’t recommend this firm. (I hope I don’t face any retaliation for this review).

I was privileged to have Attorney Joseph Reibel in my corner through 3 different claims that overlapped. I first met him after suffering a work related injury which was settled in my favor. My employer's self-insured carrier attempted to deny my claim but Mr. Reibel's work prevailed on the side of justice, and my claim was eventually settled in my favor. While this was in process, and I was recovering from surgery related to this injury, I was rear-ended by someone texting and not paying attention to traffic. Less than 8 months later, another driver, pulled out in front of me resulting in yet another motor vehicle accident. One accident on top of another, suffering from multiple injuries and leaving me in a panic as to how to deal with all of this, Mr. Reibel agreed to additionally take on the 2 MVAs. He worked diligently and professionally to settle these claims as well, even though they were not work related. Joey has always taken the time to keep me informed of what was taking place, what the next steps were, keeping me in a positive mind set, and taking care of business. We just settled both claims with these drivers' insurance. It wasn't an easy or quick task, but he made it happen, and I came out on top. I cannot thank Mr. Reibel enough and would recommend him to anyone needing his legal services.

reviews.text: If you even debate going through a bankruptcy or any law dispute for that matter, the law office of Mr. Ken Schneider should be your number one choice, they certainly were for me. Being 23 and not having much knowledge or experience in the world of debt, I was terrified of filing for bankruptcy, but after the first visit I knew we were in good hands. Ken is kind and the most generous person, and after a wonderful experience, I wouldn’t ever think to chose anyone else to help us. I seriously cannot stress how amazing this process was with them, they easily explained the process, answers all of my many questions, and were with us every step of the way, not once did we feel they didn’t have our backs. And even after our bankruptcy had been discharged, Ken is still so kind as to answer more of my questions and concerns! I have never left a review on google before but after such an amazing relationship with the law office of Ken Schneider, it’s literally impossible not to leave a review.

I want to say THANK YOU to Ken for his patience, time, and just making a very bad time in my life easier. If you find yourself in this situation and need the services of an attorney - you won't go wrong choosing Ken Schneider. All the reviews here before me were the reason I chose him and so glad I did! Now I get to add mine. He really is the best! Ken, Thank You, Thank You, Thank You!
During my free consultation with Ken, he took the time to understand my situation, before giving me advice. If I do decide to file bankruptcy, I will definitely choose him.

I had hired Ken in 2009. Ken did a fabulous job walking us through everything and actually following through. I am so happy we found him. I had recently called him for some advise and he was happy to help. I would highly recommend him. Thank you Ken.

I found Ken via the internet, and liked his reviews. From our first meeting on he made me feel comfortable. His understanding of bankruptcy law and my unique situation was a perfect fit. I felt informed, supported and his sense of humor was so welcome, made things seem less ominus. Ken made the process painless, smooth, and not nearly as bad as I thought it would be. And, he liked my dogs.

I went through my first and hopefully my last bankruptcy last spring.  I was referred to Ken by a friend who had used him in chapter 7 bankruptcy.   I was going through divorce and child custody battle, and so I needed to look at all my financial options.  What I liked most about Ken was that he never tried to push me toward bankruptcy.  He never “sugar coated” any of the negative side filing BR.  He was honest about the process and the time it would take for my credit to recover.  He’s also very knowledgeable about financial matters in general and was open to questions that I had which fell outside the realm of the BR.  For example, he gave me some great suggestions about how to better manage my one rental property that I planned to keep.  Overall, Ken knows his job well and he’s reasonably priced.  He’s honest and straightforward. I strongly recommend him.

We have worked with Ken for almost three years.  We were in a very bad financial situation, and he gave us great advice and helped throughout the entire process. He was always kind, efficient, professional and we are grateful we worked with him.

Pete Mazzone and his team are truly one of a kind. Pete is type-A, aggressive, and at times can come across as blunt or even rude—but that’s exactly what makes him so effective. His sharp, no-nonsense demeanor is his secret weapon in the courtroom.

If you’re looking for an attorney to hold your hand and sugarcoat things, Pete is not your guy. But if what matters to you is results—staying out of prison and having the strongest legal defense possible—then Pete is, without question, Washington State’s best criminal attorney.

In my own case, I’ve done extensive research, read countless articles, and spoken with people who know his work. The common theme is always the same: “Pete’s a badass, a true creature of the courtroom.” I was referred to him by someone very close to me whom I deeply respect, and after interviewing other attorneys, it was clear that Pete was the only choice. His reputation is unmatched, and his experience shows in every interaction.

My own legal journey is ongoing, and it’s an uphill battle with plenty of twists and turns—but having Pete and his team in my corner gives me real hope. I feel lucky to have them representing me, and I truly believe if anyone can see me through this, it’s Pete.

If you want honesty, expertise, and the best shot at winning, don’t think twice. Pete Mazzone is the real deal.

reviews.text:
Jason and his team were recommended to me, as a personal injury attorney, by a local chiropractor that I was receiving treatment from. I wasn't sure if I needed legal representation or if I could handle the at-fault's insurance company on my own, and I didn't immediately hire Murphy Trial Law. After 3 months of the most stressful back and forth conversations leading no where with insurance and having it begin to affect my mental health, I decided to call Murphy Trial Law and discuss my situation. I wish I hadn't waited so long!
Jason was immediately considerate of my schedule, my worries and extremely transparent with his process, potential costs (I was very concerned about being 'stuck with bills' in the end), and what he felt my case deserved as a resolution.
I felt very confident having them represent my case after just one phone call going over the basic story and their process. And I made the right choice continuing with their team. Jazmin kept me up to date every step of the way and I was able to call, email and text the team anytime for timely updates or wanting questions answered.
Jason and team superseded my case's resolution expectations and I hardly had to do anything. In fact, now that I think of it, I never even made it to meet them in person... My entire case was handled electronically and via phone, due to my work schedule and having young kids.
I cannot thank Jason and team enough for taking this burden off me, negotiating a beyond fair compensation, and making this process such a positive- less scary experience. I have already recommended Murphy Trial Law to my sibling who was recently in a car crash and urged her to "do herself a favor" by letting them aleviate her situation as well.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your kind words and for sharing your experience with us! We're thrilled to hear that we could ease your stress and exceed your expectations. It's great to know Jason and Jazmin's support made the process smoother for you. Handling everything electronically was our pleasure, especially considering your busy schedule. We’re grateful for your recommendation to your sibling, and we're here to help her or anyone else in need just as we did for you. Never hesitate to reach out if you have more questions or need assistance in the future. Wishing you all the best!

I ended up winning my case, BUT when I get referred a particular attorney and my case lays in their hands I should be now relaxed knowing that my Rep will be in Court ON MY CASE, and I shouldn’t have to call around to find out what is going on with being late!! His first excuse I expected the next 3-4 were lame. I caught on that Scott over books himself and knows it!! I seen Scott in a court case the other day and guess what? He had to step out of the court room to go to another case and held up that individuals time money and efforts, this creates mass pressure on any client like myself when this happens. I make $52 hr. Oh yeah, some other guy stepped in because Scott was on another case elsewhere when my case finally got dropped, Scott nowhere to be seen!! I’d love part of my Money back actually!! I’d go a different route, make sure the attorney doesn’t pull this kind of stuff, as it is we are already stressed out, don’t need to add to it one bit!! Good luck out there!!
reviews.ownerResponse: It sounds like we got your case dismissed. That is the best possible outcome a person can get in a criminal case.  It is normal for an attorney to be scheduled in multiple court rooms at the same time. We don't have full control over when the court schedules hearings for us.  Often people are arrested and booked and we have to go to an in-custody hearing on several hours notice.  Sometimes this means running back-and-forth between a couple of courtrooms. Other times it means arranging to have an attorney cover for you. If it was predetermined that the case was going to be dismissed, it would not be unusual for me to arrange coverage and go to a hearing where I need to actually argue something. It sounds like there was a breakdown in communication here and that caused you extra stress.  I would love to hear from you so that we can figure out how to fix that in the future.

Been looking into different departments cause I've been looking to get my right to bare arms back I'm in a fixed income, I was in trouble back in 2001 I'm 49 now n it would be nice to have all my rights back as well as help in the process

Put simply, there is one more child safe than there would have been because of Kelsey Moffitt at Snoco Legal.  Hard to put a price on that.

Kelsey Moffitt was tremendous and continues to be.  She and Snoco Legal inherited an immensely overblown and blustery case that was part of a triad of family law cases, with at least three more related family law cases on top of the main triad.

Here are a few things that Kelsey has done tremendously and without which, the outcome would be worse.

Listens.  Empathizes.  Reassures.  Prepares for the worst.  Plans and strategizes for the win.  Sets expectations. Communicates. Cares.  No. Really cares.  Executes under pressure.  Wins.

I don't think I'm representative of the main clientele of Snoco Legal.  Nor have I been a voice in favor of funding community attorneys.  I'm a business owner, conservative, PhD, former pro athlete. I've owned more rental units than I've rented and I'm married to a wife who has three degrees, never divorced and none of our 'own' kids. Just the one we're guardians of.  I am not who pro-bono attorneys nor their financiers envision helping. The child who my wife and I are guardians of, is absolutely, unquestionably, why Snoco Legal needs to exist, and why it should seek to retain attorneys like Kelsey Moffitt with raw dollars and cents.  When our funds ran dry, Kelsey Moffitt and Snoco Legal became our front line against worst of the worst.

This review is for the grantors, the board, and the investor class.  I never imagined how deeply I'd appreciate the skill and heart of a social worker, paired with the mind of an experienced attorney.  Kelsey brought it all when our family law matter merited multiple protection orders for the safety of a child at the same moment that our finances vanished.  I immediately learned just how deeply I could value soft skills and empathy embodied in the same person who would stand at the bench and do legal battle.  Battle on behalf of my family against a criminal and his high-dollar attorney funded by laundered drug money.

For an empathic attorney like Kelsey, I imagine seeing attorney Brian Ashbach's name is as unwelcome a sight as any. I mention Brian because he is willing to lie, gaslight, and ruthlessly defame whoever he goes up against, on record, in court.  He besmirches the integrity of the profession of lawyers. He is entirely threatening to the point of openly mocking and laughing at me, the opposing party, in open court on record.  Tactics that have apparently led him to many victories. He is emotionally hostile to anyone who isn't agreeable to his demands. Kelsey Moffitt warned me of Brian as best in the business in overturning anti-harassment cases like the one I had filed.  If I ever find myself as a career criminal and/or the abusive party in a family law case, I will absolutely hire Brian Ashbach, and you should too.  Just pray Kelsey Moffitt of Snoco Legal isn't your opposition's attorney.

Kelsey is not a born litigator.  You won't find the most naturally talented litigators working at Snoco Legal or anywhere where you might allocate state or philanthropic resources (or in your time of need, seek their beneficence).

But what she is, to her core, is someone who cares enough to step into the ring against aggressive and successful litigators to fight for what is right, when the stakes are high.  And to do so thoughtfully, focused, and prepared to execute a winning plan.  If we're going to fund community attorneys at all, should be betting the lion's share of our dollars for max impact.  Given my recent experience with a number of other state-funded or pro-bono attorneys and organizations, I am advocating for a reallocation of funds to Snoco Legal, from wherever else they are being allocated.  Kelsey stands head and shoulders above.

What was clear in our final plan, in all its fallbacks and contingencies, is that she sees the big picture extraordinarily well, in a way her clients may not appreciate.  It benefits them and the community that Snoco Legal serves, for years into the future.

Oh, and she wins.

I cannot thank Snohomish County Legal Services enough for the peace of mind they have restored to me.  They were quick, kind, professional, and effective.  Kelsey, and everyone at SCLS, provide an invaluable service to our community. It’s businesses and people like SCLS that strive to keep our community safe and give a voice to those who otherwise would be silenced.  We are extremely grateful to have been represented by Kelsey and Snohomish County Legal Services.
reviews.ownerResponse: We are glad we were able to be of assistance!

reviews.ownerResponse: We are glad your experience at our Wills Clinic was a positive one and that we were able to assist you with this important  task.

reviews.text: People, Remember that this is a free service.  The handfull of hard working humans here aren't overpaid, cutthroat, ambulance chasers.  FIRST- Get your ducks in a row & get your issue narrowed down to Problem, Dates, times, ppl you've spoje to.  Then 2nd-Call the number given you and leave your info.  3rd- If your issue is really big and has time restraints, perhaps you should look into someplace where you pay on a sliding income scale.  The employee that answers the VOICE MSGS is Friendly, kind, empathetic and reap ppl (But not an attorney  nor able to give legal advice.) They also see ppl at the Snohomish County Courthouse in Everett on Tues-Thurs between the hours of 9am-2pm(?) It's on their OGMsg.  NO APPTS ITS ON A  FIRST COME FIRST SERVED BASIS. Be prepared to be searched by security upon entering into the area you'll be going to, too. Pay a couple dollars to park in their underground parking.  The state should really help them out by perhaps getting them some more employees. It's like they never heard of the Pandemic!  OK. STAY OUTTA TROUBLE, DON'T BREAK THE LAW and you won't need to deal with it  😁

I hear a lot of bad experiences with lawyers here but I recommend anyone who has been through subpar representation here to do your research on Jane Pak. She is a FANTASTIC CEO, and she took the time to have a thorough and excellent conversation with me about some concerns I had about two of her lawyers. She protected her employees and really highlighted how awesome it is that they do this without the pay they deserve in a way that helped me see the situation clearly and that it was similar to my own as another CEO of a nonprofit. She really highlighted star work from Adam and was an awesome advocate for her lawyer that represented me who clearly needed a lot training on some of the niche issues of my case. Though I don't think my representation deserved her generosity and it could jeopardize the safety of vulnerable people to continue to be generous with a lawyer you have to fight to defend you properly, and she has had that made excessively clear to her by myself through facts and evidence regarding this lawyer, I think it's awesome that she's willing to learn and train them in good faith and hope they can change. She ran the meeting with the utmost clarity, excellence and competence. She listened with utmost respect and treated me with the utmost respect in a way that made me feel safe speaking about a very distressing situation of revictimization by the system. I am rating this organization five stars solely for Jane Pak because she is a truly excellent CEO and she deserves to be running a five star company if she were respected duly. I hope that happens more and more for her.

To put it simply, These guys get paid the same whether you win or lose, so what does it really matter to them? That right there should tell you everything you need to know. If you're a tenant in WA state, forget everything you may have heard or read about your rights. Tenants rights are an illusion, its merely false hope, Just as these "legal services" are. Tenants have no rights, and landlord harassment and retaliation are completely legal. No matter how much proof you have to support your case, the cards are stacked against you. How do I know this?  Well, its what Snohomish county legal services taught me.
Could not get though the phone lines. Tried for 4 days

They are compationate

Didn't help me worth a squat!! Basically, they just listened to me, and then gave me advice and became someone i could call and get more advice.
But didn't show any interest or effort to going into a court date... instead said (nicely, but was actually serious) "Maybe you should go and find an attorney near that area, they should know the laws better in that city"
SCENARIO:  Low-income, Single Full-Time Father with Full Time job. We have 2 kids together (Her first 2 of 4.. My only 2). Live with me FULL-TIME during the school year for last 3 years. The kids goto mom's on all school breaks (as long as we both can afford the transportation)  This time on the 3rd month of  summer break, she files for petition (and serves) to move kids to there, 150 miles away. Nothing wrong with here (good environment, born and raised in this area, kids fed well, they get to school and social events on time and regularly, no violence, no issues), baby-momma just misses them and wants to see them more, even tho she is the one that left and moved way over there. We had been talking fine, communicating well, and even discussed (a longtime ago) the problems/ reasons of why she isn't capable of caring for them as well as I do (her words).
The person that was helping me clearly showed that i was not worth the 'time and money for travel' for her, even though it's an obvious open-and-closed case. Maybe because I'm a guy...?? Maybe because 150 miles is too far...?? I.D.K. But I never got the help AT ALL that i needed to defend myself against a situation that i had no idea was possible of happening.
**Still pending (it's Nov. 2016), but the only involvement with my case, the attorney is having, is...... Me making an appointment with them after each court date, to "guide me" to what i need to do next before court (understanding paperwork terminology,  and what classes or events to go to) until after the next court date..... that I have to go to.... alone... with out an attorney present...
And all because they are the ONLY agency to help "low-income family's", and i can't afford $100 /HR ...with a 10 hour minimum, (due before 1st meeting = $1,000 up front), and I'm sure would also count drive-time as my-time, and probably charge expenses too (food, gas, lodging)

Bob reported my wrong address to the court month after his assistant told me he would submit my new address. He also claims to have lost my most important piece of evidence and could not even offer an apology. Totally incompetent. Worth about $15 per hour in my opinion.

Horrible experience, with an extremely misogynistic person. They failed to acquire all the evidence needed to make my case before my hearing but proceeded anyway without asking for continuance. He was more interested in if my ex was hot or not than he was in winning my case. Started insulting me based on her looks. Told me I need to grow a pair... it was a mistake to hire this person

Robert Getz is a standup guy; straightforward; he's fair and honest.  Robert gives advise to his clients as if he were in your shoes.  He has the best interest of his clients in mind and goes to bat to ensure things are carried out in the most favorable manner.  He is professional and has the respect of Snohomish County court commissioners, judges, and his legal peers alike.  I would recommend Robert Gertz to anyone needing family law assistance.

Mr. Getz, Ms. Ferrier, and the rest of the team have been excellent for my family’s needs. I appreciate the straightforward, professional representation and timely responses. Mr. Getz is also the most fair billing family law attorney I’ve encountered. I highly recommend this practice.

I brought my mother who is 96 years old to see them to have her will redone. I was told to pay $400 up at the time of the first meeting which I agreed to. That was on a Wednesday afternoon I contacted them again on Monday to check the progress and was told that they had customers in front of me and they would get started on it soon. I received an email with the will to go over it with my mother fo mer approval. I called them back to let them know that it was wrong they listed an ex brother-in-law as one of my mother's dead children which is not the case it was his wife. When I mentioned the condo to them I was told they didn't even know she had a condo and how are they supposed to know. I was in transfer to the attorney who told me I could not be in the room when she did the will which I'd already understood. I was then told they would give my 96-year-old mother half her money back which they did. I asked them to send a note to my mother explaining to her why she only gets half her money back and no will. I was then told that they will have no further contact with me. If they will do this to a 96-year-old woman I guess that pretty much makes everybody open game for them. I only wish there was something somebody could do to stop this person from doing things like this to vulnerable people.
Attorney Getz and Amy made this visiteasy to understand and they answered all the questions that my friends had.I recommend them to anyone who needs an attorney they're awesome 👍😊 Thank you

Robert Getz was very intelligent in his handling of the complexities of my family law case. Unraveling the layers of deception that previous lawyers had found insurmountable. Robert was able to bring a truthful resolution to a case that spanned nearly a decade.  We now have an Order with the safeguards for my family that allow us to move forward to a successful future.  Thank you from all us.
Bob was great. Always there to answer any questions I had. Would definitely redommend.

WORST LAWYER (or any professional) I'VE EVER DELBT WITH!!!! Incredibly dissatisfied with services. A mediation to sort out a summer schedule was drawn out to be SIX LONG HOURS! Phone conversations that should have taken 10-15 min lasted 45 min to an hour because he told stories of how things were years ago, previous cases, explained things I already knew, chit chat that had nothing to do with the case. Then to be "nice" he would knock 10 min off the bill. I spent over $6000 to mediate 9 weeks of summer. I discussed with Mr. Getz that I did not want to pay for any mediation cost as my X was requesting it all. After it was all done I was told I had to pay 1/2 of the mediation bill and was told by the mediator that they sent me/my lawyer a letter advising that it would be 50/50 in advance. I never received a letter. I called Getz receptionist after and asked her to please forward me a copy of that letter and she told me there was never a letter. My impression of Mr Getz is that he is interested in making things long and drawn out so he makes as much money as he can, not interested in helping his clients solve situations with limited financial damages. I spent more money sorting out this one part of my parenting plan then I spent creating the entire parenting plan.

Bob has now saved me from financial exposure to almost $90k in potential judgment orders through his careful, meticulous and detailed knowledge of case law and the local legal system! When it comes to finding an attorney, don't make the same mistake I did and shop solely by price because in law the smallest mistake will cost YOU, not your attorney, dearly! Bob is worth every penny and then some... the proof is in the results and Bob gets results!

Fabian

It is hard to say I loved the legal experience but having Mr. Getz on my side made a huge different from my 1st attorney. Mr. Getz gave me back my parental rights that I lost with my first attorney.  Robert Getz is not only a very good lawyer, he is someone I can call a friend now.

So far my experience with Lucas Barringer one of their attorneys has been fantastic and I think he's doing a great job for me and I think he's going to really help me with my cases and even though I know he has a huge case load when he's talking to you he's focusing on you and only you and I really appreciate that

No for being just public defenders made you the job of a paid lawyer they are courteous and they really are there for you. Thank you for working for the community you guys never get the credit you deserve.

Such a great firm. I wish I could give more stars!

If I do, I'd call Mr. Jolly and his office again without question.  The Law Firm of David Jolly is the best in their business and I'm so glad I had them to help me through this. Thank you, David!

Terry is an excellent immigration attorney. I would 100% recommend working with her, as the entire process was quick, clear, and simple. Thanks to her up-to-date knowledge of immigration issues and her suggestions, I received my visa in approximately two weeks.

reviews.text:
While we ultimately didn’t move forward with Carson Law Group, I felt like I needed to leave a review because of our experience. After reviewing our situation, Holly was honest in telling us that she didn’t believe we had a case. That kind of integrity is incredibly valuable and deserves recognition.

It would have been easy to take our money and move forward anyway, but instead she chose to be straightforward and transparent. Out of six attorneys we contacted, Carson Law Group was the only one who took the time to respond and let us know we didn’t have a case. We truly appreciated the honesty and the time they gave us. It’s reassuring to know there are still good, ethical professionals out there.
Frank gave an extensive consultation. He ended up resolving my tenant issue question and gave recommendations without needing to  proceed further. I would go with Frank in the future if/when needed.

Holly did a fantastic job handling my mom’s estate. There were many moving parts, and surprisingly, I had a hard time finding an attorney who could take this on. After nearly four months of searching and speaking with multiple lawyers, I finally found Holly and I’m grateful I did.

From start to finish, Holly did exceptional work and continues to help me navigate follow-up questions related to my mom’s estate. I’ve known many attorneys over the years, including those recognized on “top lawyer” and national lists. Holly is that caliber of lawyer.

She is personable, patient, thoughtful, and extremely competent. Having been in my own profession for over twenty-five years, I know how to recognize excellence and Holly is truly excellent at what she does. She is also humble and genuinely desires to help people.

If you are looking for a first-rate estate attorney, look no further than Holly Shannon. You will be well-represented, well-cared for, and glad you chose her.

While searching for an attorney for a squatter issue, Frank returned my call right away. He was very kind and spoke with me regarding the issue for quite some time. Unfortunately we didn't get to work together but he would be first on my list to call when an attorney is needed. He has a great voice too.

Lawsuits are rarely pleasant, especially when you're on the receiving end. That was my reality for a while, facing off against Carson Law Group PLLC. It felt like an uphill battle against the world. However, I'm leaving this 5-star review because of my positive interactions with the lawyer Francis the friendly receptionist and some other nice lawyer guy (ill explain below) anyways, despite the stress of the situation, I always felt respected. Whether dropping off documents, attending court, or speaking on the phone, I was greeted with kindness and professionalism. Francis was focused on the case, but I never felt belittled or dismissed.
One Friday evening, I realized I'd left my phone in the office after dropping off paperwork. It was the weekend, and I panicked. I rushed back to the office, hoping someone was still there. (🤫all laws being obeyed of course 🫣 lol)
The lights were off, but I saw a car backing out. I took a chance, running up to the window and (probably looking like a frantic freak) asking if he could check for my phone.
He patiently parked, escorted me inside, and helped me search. When we couldn't find it, he walked me back to my car, where I realized it was sitting on my seat! Mortified, I thanked him profusely, and he simply smiled and wished me a good weekend. (I Assumed another Lawyer in the office; I've never met him prior but he wore a suit and HELD THE KEYS to get in)
Who does that? Most people, especially in an adversarial situation, would have been less accommodating. But everyone at Carson Law Group, PLLC consistently demonstrated genuine kindness and professionalism.
I know if I ever need legal representation, Francis will be my first call.

Holly and Frank are smart, sharp, hard-working and calculated. They won't waste your time and money -- they focus on what's important and keep your case moving forward. We can't reccomend Carson Law highly enough -- they are absolute bosses (and also relatable, approachable humans). Fantastic to work with.

I am one of the partners of Carson Law Group, PLLC. I write in response to the comment made by "Leslie Thomas" below. After a review of our files, I can confirm that this individual was never a client or prospective client of our firm. As is sometimes the case, parties on the opposite side of a legal matter will be frustrated with the effective work performed by us on behalf of our clients. We believe "Cherie R" to be such a person, but he or she is electing to use a false identity to avoid accountability for his or her statements. Accordingly, on behalf of my team here at Carson Law Group, PLLC, I respectfully request that his or her comment be disregarded. Should you be in need of competent and cost-effective legal services, please do not hesitate to contact us.
I have always received prompt responses to inquiries, knowledgeable advice and helpful recommendations. I appreciate the attorney's attention to detail and vast experience.
Horrible and slow service.
Very satisfied with timely done quality work.
Great advice and representation! I would recommend to anyone needing legal assistance.

All of the negative reviews appeared the day after a contentious court hearing with a pro se defendant.  While we appreciate the opportunity to respond to negative reviews, that task is impossible when the poster is anonymous.  None of the posts appear to have come from clients.  All of the posts appear to have been made simply to cause harm to our business.  So we have decided to post this and request that the posts be removed.  If the posts are not removed then we intend to seeking the identity of the individuals who posted the reviews.  If they are in fact clients, then we will work with our clients to correct any errors we have made.  If they are not clients then we will consider other appropriate courses of action.
was disgusted with the service and how the way he treated me and my family. Not worth your time and money

I was surprised to see the negative post by the prior poster.  Mr. Carson does not practice law in the State of California where she is located.  Mr. Carson has never undertaken a divorce case.  So perhaps the review by this person was in error?

reviews.text:
Bill Tri is very rude. Took a year for a settlement. We moved out of state, settled a month ago and STILL NO CHECK! Said it was mailed but they gave no proof of service or tracking number. Stay clear of this law firm. Very judgemental and rude.
UPDATE: Cassandra his assistant is very incompetent and rude. Told me that my parents could not pick up the check, only to find out that is not true. She sent the check out to the wrong address so I had to pay $25 for them to reissue it all for them to put the incorrect amount on the check. Now I am waiting for another "check"!
I was extremely impressed with the service that I received from everyone at the firm.  Very reasonable prices and they earn every dollar.  I highly recommend them to everyone.

Mr Tri is a very bad attorney.  He lies by omission and knows what he is doing.  He is very good at lying by omission.

I am not going to mention names here because the firm is aware of who represented me, but the whole experience did not meet my expectations. Several people who were aware of my communications with my counsel were shocked at how unsupportive they were at times. They were also not prepared for the complexities of my case but did not suggest finding more qualified counsel instead, even after I asked if I should consider other options. Unfortunately, I wasn’t able to find alternate counsel by the time I realized how bad the situation was and a change at that point would have put an agreement, that I ended up having to negotiate and structure myself, at risk of falling apart. That was made worse by the fact the case was unnecessarily extended in time and cost by not being prioritized either as needed or as advertised. I hired another firm and lawyer in parallel for a separate family law matter and the experience was night and day different in terms of competence, professionalism, and empathy. Aside from my subjective experience, they charged me to fix at least one mistake that was caused by the firm, which feels borderline unethical. I contacted the firm’s principal directly and did seem to see some improvement following that, but never got a direct response or any kind of contrition. When I paid my final balance and gave feedback about the concerns I had with how the case was wrapped up, I got no response from my counsel (the paralegal did thoroughly look into and provide context on one of my two concerns, to their individual credit). In my professional line of work this would be completely unacceptable behavior. To be fair, the case did turn out to be more complex than I first expected, but if you are in over your head you ask for help or admit you can’t handle it instead of dragging clients along.

After 5 years hiring multiple attorneys in an attempt to bring resolution in a nasty divorce case, I finally contacted Brewe Layman and within 30 days it was resolved.  Brewe Layman was easily the best firm I've hired.  They were the most attentive, easily the best value and by far and away the most effective.  Thank you, Kelli.  Also, to all of the Brewe Layman partners and employees, I give my thanks and gratitude.
What a horrible, horrible person. I’d give zero stars if I could. I am still in shock over how I was treated. Would not recommend to anyone.
Do not work with Matthew D Taylor. He stole 10k from me, signed me up for an unfair alimony, and then ghosted me. He only cares about himself.
I have nothing but good things to say about Robert Miller and the team at Brewe Layman. Everything was handled promptly, thoroughly, and professionally.

Their website clearly states BUSINESS LAW "From partnership, corporation and LLC formation...", "Kenneth Brewe has more than 40 years experience dealing with routine and complicated business matters including litigation. If you have a Business Law need, please contact us to set up a consultation", but when calling their office, the person on the phone says that they do NOT do that at all, she was absolutely surprised of our inquiry, and apparently has never seen their website and unable to check it to see they supposedly do Business Law!
Very professional and timely, Lacy and Tammy actually care about you as a human being. Incredible people and amazing work ethic, so thankful to have had them!

Amazing attorneys and staff!! Couldn't ask for anything more. I've had several lawyers help me during the last 10 years fighting for custody and this law firm truly delivered. I would recommend Rebecca to anyone dealing with a difficult and complicated case. I'm so impressed by this firm and cannot say enough good things about them. They are worth every penny and then some!
Great services fast & easy highly recommend them for all your needs.
I reached out to several different law firms to adopt my son in law. No one responded back. And I am thankful they didn't. I have had the best experience ever. I would recommend over and over again.

Do not recommend using this firm. I’m just going to highlight the unprofessional moments that really made me lose any confidence I had in this firm. After going through mediation and trying to sign the agreement it was a total mess. They messed up the paperwork too many times to count, they would send it back and there where errors. So I would have to point out that it didn’t match the CR2 agreement. They didn’t even spell my name right, not even on the final paperwork after I asked them to fix it. The court date had to keep getting moved back because they paperwork kept having so many errors. Which fine I get it we all make mistakes, but they charged me another 2,800 after mediation because of the delays and they weren’t my fault. And that charge just hit my credit card without any bill being sent to me explaining what the services rendered the charge, I didn’t get that until I called the billing department. They claimed it was an email issue, funny though because my email works just fine when the paralegal would send me “updates” and then tried to charge me another 1,200 once again without a statement explaining the services provided. I get it if you are talking with the Attorney formally and they are giving you professional legal advice yes you have to pay for that. But I lost my mind when I was being charged for errors of their end and the lack of sticking to a timeline. I did what I was supposed to now do your job. They did refund the last charge, but only after I called the paralegal out on it. I wanted to talk with my attorney but he was on vacation or something. And there was no apology or explanation. Once again they claimed it was an automatic billing error. But had I not said anything they would have just kept charging me and dragging out my case. And no I’m not happy with how the parenting plan worked out, I didn’t feel represented during mediation, I felt like I gave up a lot more then I had to. But hey that’s just the legal document that is binding to my kids and I for the next 14 years. If you want someone to pay attention to your case don’t go with this firm. You may as well pay yourself because you’ll have to do all the leg work while they charge you for brash e-mails with little substance leaving you confused and more stressed out about your divorce.  I had Rhonda Johnson and Bob Miller.
reviews.ownerResponse: No legal matter is ever pleasant, but we strive to provide our clients with clear and honest discussions regarding your legal matter, even though sometimes it's not what you were hoping to hear.  Brewe Layman is sorry that you had a frustrating legal experience and are concerned to learn of your dissatisfaction. Many of our clients have posted positive reviews and were very happy with both the outcome of their matter, and with our ability to help them through the confusing and emotional process of family law litigation. We would value a discussion with you to determine how and why your experience differed from those who gave us 5 stars.

reviews.text: Ken and Debbie were AMAZING for me during my divorce. 34 year marriage,  and i was clueless how it would all work. Fairness and respect was important to me, and they made it happen. Im friends with my ex, which wouldn't have been possible had this been long, drawn out and mean spirited. I would 110% recommend Brewe Layman.

Bob Miller was my lawyer who came recommend to me. He was the worst recommendation I have ever had. I needed to move as I could no longer stay in the area due to financial hardship. Bob did not stand up for me in mediation. He sat there laughing and joking with the mediator Elizabeth Turner the entire 8 hours.  These two would not mediate at all. Instead of negotiations, Elizabeth and Bob would act as if they were a judge often stating  "this is what a judge would've decided". My ex didn't even submit any paperwork to the mediator prior to mediation. It was like the mediator was making inferences towards me and my case. I wasn't asking for the parenting plan to be changed completely, as I want me kids to enjoy time with their father. I figure she should not be so bias since she didn't have any documents from my ex. Bob didn't seem to care that my ex didn't supply any documentation, Bob just agreed with everything Elizabeth Turner had to say.  In the end, I lost. Just because I decided to move 30 miles away. I had my children 70% of the time before all of this. Now I see them for 6 days out of each month.  I have read another persons review of Bob which was similar to mine, except his ex-wife moved out of state and got to keep the kids. Please avoid Bob. He is literally here to be a life/ money suck and he'll make sure you won't get to see your kids as often as you use to.
Ken and Rhonda are the best! I can't say enough about them. Great representation and always professional & quick to responsed👍

Even though these guys get paid by the state, they do seem to care about the case in front of them and will do what they can for you.  Bottom line is that these guys/gals tend to represent their clients as genuinely as they can, & the law allows, seemingly moreso out of most of the Public Defenders in the county.
