reviews.text: The team at Lauber Law Group was great. My case demanded time and patience and they stuck with me the whole way!
These ladies worked really hard to help me take care of a tricky legal issue.  They are very complete and got results.  Very sweet and professional, a deadly combination.

I never set foot in their office. Every interaction was through phone or email and yet, somehow, it felt like home.

From the very beginning, the team treated me with kindness, empathy, and zero judgment. I was terrified, overwhelmed, and honestly didn’t know if I could even go through with my case — but they walked me through every step with patience and respect. I wasn’t just a case number; I felt seen. Like they truly cared.

They never made me feel small for asking questions (and trust me, I had a lot). They responded quickly, explained things clearly, and made what felt like an impossible situation actually doable. There were moments I wanted to cry just from the relief of being taken care of so well.

Now that it’s over, I’m weirdly emotional. I’m so grateful, and honestly kind of sad to be done with this chapter. But if I ever need help again, I won’t think twice — I’ll come straight back.

This isn’t just a law office. This is where you go when you need someone to help you feel human again.

Thank you for everything.  You have no idea what your kindness,  your work and dedication meant to me and my family.  I will forever be grateful for crossing paths.

I recently had the opportunity to work with Lauber Law Group for my bankruptcy case, and I couldn’t be more grateful for their help during such a difficult time.

From the very first consultation, they made me feel understood and supported. The team was professional, responsive, and incredibly knowledgeable. They took the time to explain every step of the process in clear, simple terms, and they were always available to answer my questions or concerns — no matter how small.

What I appreciated most was their compassion and patience. Bankruptcy is a stressful and emotional experience, but the staff at Lauber Law Group handled my case with empathy and without judgment. I truly felt like they had my best interest at heart.

Thanks to their guidance and hard work, my case was handled smoothly and efficiently, and I finally have a fresh start. If you're looking for a trustworthy and experienced law firm to help with your bankruptcy or financial matters, I highly recommend [Law Group Name].
They was very good with communication and organization. My case got filed and approved in a timely manner. Very happy that I was able to work with them on my case.
Very reliable, quick, professional
Super easy, fast and efficient. Follow up immediately and always getting a response!

When my mother passed away last year up in Washington, I was facing a situation where I was expected to be the executor of the estate from California, and there was no will or trust. In those first few weeks it was quite overwhelming, and I'm so thankful that I found Lauber Law Group and had a chance to work with Jaclyn Bray. From the beginning, Jacki was so efficient and on top of it. Within a week of meeting her I was appointed by the court, and we were off and running. Estate bank account, property liquidation, selling a house, so many steps along the way and she was able to guide me through each of them with no hiccups with everything done remotely. Thank you Jacki, there is no way I would have made it through this last year without your guidance and expertise.

One thing I don’t tolerate as a client and in this case, an even potential client; is dishonesty and wasting my time. I submitted for a consultation through a website and was contacted by this group, a consultation was set up. I took the time off work, made sure my children had a sitter, and sat at my desk for 39 mins and never received a phone call of any kind. Instead I received a call this morning and was instantly accused of missing my appointment, that the woman “called me,” and “left me a voicemail.” I immediately interrupted the person whom called me and informed them this information was inaccurate that I never received a call, and my voicemail is actually full so they never would have been able to “leave me a message,” as they allegedly claimed. I decided right then and there it was too much a red flag to even proceed to try to continue, I politely explained I wouldn’t need their services as I value my time. If the group wants to argue these facts, I’ll let them, and then follow up with screenshots for proof once my cell records are posted for this contact date.
reviews.ownerResponse: Ms. Quintal, thank you for giving us the opportunity.  We would make every attempt to resolve this misunderstanding, particularly as it was an after-hours consultation where staff was not available to facilitate the connection, but we believe this would ultimately not have been a mutually beneficial relationship at this juncture.  We do wish you the best of luck going forward.

I got a call back and saying I'll like to file a complaint and was told I'm not going to get into this again and was hung up on once again.

We went ahead and withdrew so he can find an attorney who is looking to serve only one client at a time.  We wish him the best of luck.

reviews.text: Everyone was so helpful in helping me through a challenging time in my life. Thank you for everything you did for me

I would recommend Jaclyn Bray of Lauber Law Group to anyone going through the divorce process or wanting to improve their estate planning.  Jaclyn represented me in a complicated divorce. She was very communicative, responsive, respectful, and fair through the whole process.  She provided sound advice and helped me stay grounded as we worked through my case.   She is now helping me with estate planning and has been fantastic on this front as well.  She is top notch and would benefit anyone by representing them.

Thanks to our awesome attorney at the Lauber law group.

I generally do not write negative reviews, and am never unfair.  If you are seeking quality legal representation, steer as clear as you possibly can from this firm.

In the five years that I have been "represented" (that's a joke) by Lauber Law Group (FKA Lauber Dancey), I have gone through experiences that are borderline unethical.  I will be submitting a complaint to the Bar as well.  Here are some my experiences:

- I hired Delaina Dancey, a partner, to handle my case.  I received ineffective guidance that led to delays in my case, and less than desired outcomes.

- After months of delays, I prepared all of my own paperwork, and Ms. Dancey was rude and argumentative with any of my questions or concerns about her approach.  When she finally had her "paralegal" (who changed 3x in 1 year) file my case, things moved forward.

- Ms. Dancey arrived late and unprepared to all hearings and appearances, and even called my spouse and I by the wrong names.  During any disagreements or questions by the opposing party, she was most focused on ease and speed, rather than the best-interest of her clients.

- When my orders were finally entered and the case was underway, the firm was paid in full, and future emails and calls went unanswered for weeks.

- I had a situation that required an appearance in court, and though she charged me an additional $1,000, Ms. Dancey no-showed the telephone hearing and emailed me hours later to tell me that the hearing was unnecessary.  The court had already ruled in my favor (based on the paperwork I submitted on my own).

- Months later, I had a change in circumstances that required legal advice.  After 5 weeks of emailing and calling, I learned from Ashley Lauber that Ms. Dancey had retired without notice.  This is not ethical.  I was referred to a "friend" of Ms. Lauber's who... "wasn't my attorney, but would help out."

- The substitute attorney managed to file a few forms that, again, were completed 100% by me, and my case went on.

- With 6 months remaining in my case, I had another change in circumstances and had no idea how to proceed with my case.  I asked my substitute attorney questions, and she voiced concerns that she may not be able to represent me due to potential "conflict."  I rephrased the questions as what they were... general policy information questions, on behalf of both of her clients, that we needed answers to on how to proceed.  The substitute provided some information that was unclear.

- When I asked for clarity, Ms. Lauber abruptly responded to let me know that she and her substitute were withdrawing from my case.

If you want your case handled professionally and you are looking for an advocate who will support you and work in your best interest, avoid this firm at all costs!
reviews.ownerResponse: In recent weeks, after five years of consistent and favorable representation, Mr. Sugarman presented us with a conflict of interest regarding his marital community.  There are ethical lines that professional attorneys simply will not cross, especially while representing more than one person.  We will not go into detail regarding the case itself here, except to say that we provided many hours of pro bono legal work post-petition, were attentive to his needs and requests, and we dispute all of his accusations.  We are sympathetic to the financial and personal stresses that drive an individual to needing legal assistance, and it is not unusual for a client to project their personal unhappiness onto their attorneys.   We are sorry that Mr. Sugarman is disappointed, but we stand by our decision to withdraw.  We wish Mr. and Mrs. Sugarman the best of luck going forward.

reviews.text: We just recently hired this firm and had the absolute worst experience ever! We never even spoke to an actual attorney. Over 11,000 in less than a month to have his office manager playing lawyer with our family law case. She misfiled and misrepresented us terribly. It doesn’t appear to me that This firm cares about people seems they just want your money. We dropped them and went to Michael Gallagher. He is shocked at the mishandling of this case. He has to start completely over. I would NOT HIRE THIS FIRM. I think you’re better off representing yourself. In my opinion they are crooks! Natascha is not an attorney. We are contacting the bar because what they have done to our kids is traumatizing and so messed up.

Jermaya is Very unprofessional I went his office in 2016 for my asylum application he even didn’t  know the immigration process instead guiding me he was asking me what to do and also he couldn’t attend my first master hearing I went in court by myself which caused me not having SSN and driving license for 6 years I do not recommend Jermaya  to anyone for immigration process.

At this point I will give a review and another when is needed,Styles law listened to my concerns about my personal injury and civil claim dilegently and respectfully added my wife and I to there law firm as clients and started our journey toward real legal representation.good job you guys, finally and prayerfully our children and I will get real compensation.

Excellent service! The lawyer helped me with a car accident and kept me informed throughout the entire process. The assistants were very friendly and always answered all my questions. I highly recommend them!
They're terrible, they only know how to lie to get your money and then they just make excuses.

I do not recommend Jeremiah.

He did not let me know that he would not be showing up to my final divorce hearing. Imagine coming to the final hearing and meeting Jeremiah's associate, whom I have never even heard of before. I felt the ground has been taken away right from underneath my feet, as his associate had no idea what my case was about and could not answer questions about the paperwork filed by Jeremiah. I requested to continue the trial and fired Jeremiah and his company on the spot.
When I brought this up in an email after my hearing, he said "I let you know as soon as I could." Which is when? 15 minutes before a life-changing trial, where issues of a 3 year old child custody were being decided? The sad part - a day before the trial, his receptionist called me to ask me if I wanted to conduct the trial from home (it was early 2021), or to come into Jeremiah's office. I chose to come to the office, as I needed all the support I could get from my attorney. Well, now you know how that went.

In that email Jeremiah also said that I was not happy with my attorney before him as well. So what? Am I to believe that two unprofessional attorneys are somehow an indication of issues on my side?

He also cited an RCW that is absolutely not applicable to my case.  Factually not applicable.

The bottom line - I would not recommend to hire Jeremiah (and probably anyone else at his company, for that matter), unless you are into wasting a ton of money on unprofessional help.

- Denis

DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY! GO TO ANOTHER ATTORNEY! Jeremiah was not my attorney- he was my ex's. I told my ex over a year ago that he should find a new attorney, as Jeremiah is not responsive, not attentive, not professional, not communicative and incredibly lazy providing sub par work. Here are some of the reasons why I feel this way:

1) Jeremiah charged my ex $2000 and I am not certain what he did, as I filled out the majority of our divorce paperwork and forwarded them to him. He did almost no work for this money. I paid five times as much money for my attorney to review my documents, but you truly do get what you pay for in this field.

2) Once we had our hearing for a temp child support (that I initiated because Mr Styles opened 2 cases ith CSA and it was a mess due to a residential split), it had to be continued because Jeremiah FORGOT to request an interpreter for his Spanish speaking client. (He "specializes" in serving this community, yet forgot to order an interpreter?!)

3) Once we finally had the the temporary child support hearing, Jeremiah LIED in court multiple times. First, that he did not know my ex was present in the courtroom instead of via zoom (which he did the first hearing, as well). He never took the time to communicate with his client to ensure that he knew how to get on the zoom call. He also lied in the courtroom about my ex's occupation and told the commissioner that  he never earned income doing side landscaping work.He lied to the commissioner and stated that my ex's income had changed drastically since 2019 (which is did not, except that his bills decreased), yet did not provide any evidence about this "drastic change" because it was not true.

4) When the commissioner asked Styles why his client did not provide all of the financial documentation required by the court, he told the commissioner that he had tried to obtain it from his client and he did not submit it. He actually blamed his client. My ex did not even know what to provide.

5) In the written testimony that Styles wrote on behalf of my ex (that my ex signed), he stated that our youngest son was a "daughter". In the proposed child support worksheets, Jeremiah had 3 other people's names sprinkled throughout not associated with our case! This is unacceptable, unprofessional and extremely lazy work. As an RN that was representing myself, I had no problem winning this court case, despite my limited knowledge of law. It was only after that that he started badgering me with emails about mandatory mediation. I responded the next day with my availability and then never heard from him again.

6) Lastly, my ex and I came to an agreement and set a hearing for a pro se dissolution. Styles was emailed almost 4 weeks prior to the hearing requesting that he withdraw. he NEVER did!! This resulted in our case being thrown out and I had.to refile. This time, I emailed him prior to out final hearing and within a day or 2 he finally withdrew from the case,

In closing, Mr Styles is an attorney that I would not even wish on my ex husband nor my worst enemy I believe he gets away with being lazy because  he "serves" the Spanish speaking community and they may not know any differently if their English is limited. From what I saw, I believe he took advantage of the fact that my ex could not navigate the court system nor did he understand the English used in court documents. His communication was poor, he charged my ex when all he did was open a CSA case (two) and created a proposed order of child support with worksheets and a written testimony that were not accurate and did not have the correct names. I filled out the rest of the documentation and sent it to him. He even asked me to sent it twice because he did not want to "search his email box" for my original docs.

KEEP SEARCHING FOR AN ATTORNEY!! DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY OR YOUR TIME HERE. He did not take my money but his sloppy work and lack of communication/follow through with my ex did cost me a lot of extra money, time and energy. I also plan on filing a complaint wit the Bar Association.
Just perfect

reviews.text: I live in North Carolina and Christina Faliero has been very willing to help me in my quest to be treated faily by my employer! She is willing to go the last mile to assist her clients! Thanks for all that you do! It's very comforting to know that your rights will be protected and well represented while working with this firm! Trust and believe it's very hard to find a law firm that's willing to go up against USPS! Thanks!
Great Firm, I've done a lot of Business with Peter. Always knowledgeable and extremely responsive. They have a great Team here at Galloway Law group that keeps companies like ours protected! Thanks
Peter is a bulldozer ! Extremely thorough, honest and hard working. Very well spoken as you would expect from an attorney but he doesn’t waste time. He’s intimidating, yet very likable as a human.

Man, I was at a standstill in my case. I needed representation, and many lawyers wouldn't call back after I stated my case, or wouldn't even call to figure out the case after leaving a voicemail. I was at wits end almost and Adam Strand took my case. He was very professional, very well spoken. He helped me see how my case could turn out, even though it was messy and he couldn't guarantee a outcome, he told me he will do his very best to help me. And that, he has been doing. I knew nothing about law, and he guided me. My case is seeing the light at the end of the tunnel for me and my children. I recommend full heartedly, 1,000%, his services. If I ever need anything that he is able to do, I'm going back for sure. Thank you again Adam. You're truly a great lawyer.

I would not recommend Adam Strand as legal representation based on my personal experience. His communication was incredibly poor—he consistently failed to explain things in a way that I could understand, leaving me confused and unsupported during a critical time. Rather than feeling guided, I often felt judged and even attacked by the way he spoke to me.

Beyond the communication issues, there were serious concerns with how he handled my case. Motions were filed improperly, which ultimately harmed my position and delayed progress. It became clear that there was no real advocacy happening on my behalf—just a drain on my resources.

Thankfully, after switching to a lawyer who genuinely fought for my case, we were able to make meaningful progress and reach a resolution. The contrast was stark. I regret the time and money spent with Adam Strand and would advise others to seek representation elsewhere.
Thanks for y'alls help

Peter and his team are both friendly and professional.   They were very effective on my real estate issue and also put my estate planning together.   Absolutely a good relationship to have for legal assistance for this world we live in.
5/5 stars for Edward Moore and his team on a topic surrounding real estate. Great depth of knowledge and guidance. I would recommend Edward and his team any day.

Edward Moore really let my friend down. She provided every scrap of information he requested for their case in Superior Court. The trial was scheduled months in advance but it appears he waited until the week before to prep. That's when he started asking questions anyway. He failed to answer our status inquires or communicate his needs to my friend no matter how often she tried to communicate with him over the months. My friend was deposed but he decided to not deposed the opposition. It appears he just ran out of time. He showed up in court unprepared, failed to challenge outlandish statements by the opposition or even ask questions that were discussed ahead of time. My friend compiled a library of over 100 videos and provided them. He didn't show any of them and when asked he said he "forgot". He also forgot to ask key questions. Obviously we lost the case but this is NOT sour grapes. It's what I personally observed in the courtroom. A very disappointing experience. Glad he wasn't my lawyer.

And of course, months after this comment, no response from the company. I'm sure they will pitch a fit if their payment is delayed.

A few years back, I had Bruce and his team work with me on several contract/lease issues (in the Snohomish area) for our church there.   I have since moved down to SW Washington.  I did not hesitate to reach out to Bruce regarding contract negotiations (with Verizon) on possible cell tower lease options on our current church’s property.  His experience has helped tremendously even though I am no longer “local”.  I have always felt that he has the client’s best interest in mind.
Awful probate firm.  Drags on forever.   Should have closed months ago,  but they dont do anything... like normal filings unless you get on them and then they charge you for the phone call.
Very helpful and courteous.

Adam Strand is an exceptionally poor attorney and even worse at communication. Don’t expect him to come to trial prepared or have him answer emails. He will be very prompt to send an invoice but any concern you have will go completely ignored. He wont work with other attorneys to settle anything and prefers to go to court entirely unprepared and just wing it since it increases his payment. I can’t say if more experience will help him or not but now he basically doesn’t take any time to do the work or even concern himself with your issues. He seems to have a lack for understanding that he is practicing family law and this is people's lives he's gambling with. Don't even want to give one star.

I have recently have had the pleasure of working with Peter Gallagher of the Galloway Law Group PLLC. He wrote my father's will as well as did the probate for my parent's estate. It was not easy, considering there was some push-back from the other side of the family, if you know what I mean. Peter was vigilante in his representation of us and he went above and beyond of what was required of him.. He spoke with the opposing parties attorney on a continual basis and kept us informed of any and all changes as they occurred. We were very pleased with his services, as well as his fees. To be honest, he is is still working on this case to this day, however we are hoping to get this wrapped up within a very short time. If you are looking to have a Will drawn up, Estate Planning, Probate, whatever the case may be regarding property, etc. I cannot recommend this man more. He is the the best lawyer that I can think of in the Lake Stevens Area, if not all of Snohomish County.
Thank you,
Dani G.

If I could give them a negative rating I would. My 81 year old mother went to them for advise on an eviction. They sent her home with a form to sign and return with a $5000 retainer. I flipped my lid and we contacted another attorney who did what we needed for $124.00 - my mother then receive a bill for over $400 for her 1.33 hour consultation.

Buyer be ware - they are also working on another case for my family and it's already taking longer than expected as well.

When I read the other person's comment about their probate experience I am seeing the same thing with ours....

My wife has been dealing with this law firm to handle probate on my mother in laws house since she passed away last year. Bruce Galloway is by far the worst lawyer I've ever had experience with, he did not adequately answer any questions and his rate for a probate case was way above any other law firm i have spoken to since. They routinely forgot to give paperwork amd were just overall a poor excuse for a law firm. I will make sure any of my friends and family do not come here for any legal means because they will screw you.

Galloway Law Group recently handled an escrow and closing for me on the sale of my mixed usage building. This sale should have been a relatively straight forward transaction with me carrying the note. However, the closing of this transaction and final settlement were filled with errors and had been poorly mishandled by both my real estate agent and the large escrow company.
Bruce and his staff were able to correct the information and large mistakes previously made by others. Bruce, Sarah, Caiti and Carmen were a pleasure to deal with. They kept me informed on every aspect of this closing and completed it very professionally
I highly recommend the Galloway Law Group

reviews.text: Had 2 consultations,.one in March and one in April.  Never heard anything back. Made a call and they said they would get back to me but nothing. Cant rember when thay was. Called again in October and the same. Called to pick up my documents in november and the assistant said she would call back but nothing. Got the documents a week later. They didn't start working on my stuff till July. And didn't touch it again till November to bill me 2700.00 dollars. I have pictures of the bill for proof. Unfortunately all the documents have to be redone as when we started the process in march we advised them of a name change in april and they did not make that update. Just someone else make your life easier.

I have never written a review before, but I felt compelled to do so now because of how well Melanie represented me during the worst time of my life. Melanie starts by meeting with you and laying out different options and strategies that you can pursue . She was very honest about what you could expect throughout your time with her. Watching Melanie litigate on your behalf in the courtroom is truly something special. She's very fierce in her arguments but gentle in how it comes across. On more than one occasion, lawyers from different firms asked for her advice. She is highly respected by her colleagues and the courts. The bottom line is that Melanie will work tirelessly and effectively for you.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Renee DeFreece has been an invaluable source of support and guidance for our family over the past year. Her expertise and dedication in handling our complex family affairs have been nothing short of exceptional.

Renee's guidance has been instrumental for our family. Her proactive approach, attention to detail, and excellent communication skills made the entire process as smooth as possible. She genuinely cares about her clients' well-being and is always there to address any concerns or questions.

If you are in need of a lawyer who will go above and beyond to ensure the best outcome for your family, look no further than Renee DeFreece. We are incredibly fortunate to have had her on our side. Thank you, Renee, for your outstanding service!

Melanie is THE lawyer you want. She saved me from a pretty big hole I dug myself by being in denial at my divorce was going to happen. She had brilliant strategies and never let me get sucked into the narrative by the other side. I ended up with a rather favorable settlement and it's 100% to Melanie's credit. She will not steer you wrong. Thank you, Melanie for all your hard work.

I have worked with three of the attorneys (Melanie, Deane, and Tina) on a non parental custody case, an adoption case, estate planning, and settling an estate. This team is so professional, committed, and well-respected. It is so important to go into court with an attorney that the commissioners and judges know and trust. I’ve seen first hand the difference in how the court views your case with run of the mill counsel. I never imagined when we started working with them that their whole team would show up at court for our adoption trial. It shows personal and professional dedication that I believe is a rare commodity. I cannot recommend them enough.

I cannot express my gratitude and happiness to the entire team at the firm. Deane, Amy, Tina and Kara took the hardest year of our lives and made it as simple and clear as it could have possibly been. Forever grateful and will suggest them to anyone who needs legal council, specifically family law.

I met Renee about two ago and immediately found her to be not only incredibly smart but also someone with so much compassion and empathy.  I highly recommend working with her and having her by your side.
Renee was great, very responsive. Would definitely recommend her and use again if needed.

Renee made my divorce as easy and painless as possible.  Much easier than I could imagine and she took the time to listen to my concerns and explained everything so I could understand but also professional enough that I fully trusted working with her. Renee took care of everything so I didn't have to.  I would highly recommend using Renee.

I had the pleasure of working with Renee as she assisted us in doing an adult adoption! I could have no asked for things to go more seamless and smooth, she communicated everything we need to do and helped us every step of the way. at the last minute the court changed it to a zoom meeting instead of an in court meeting and and she rearranged her office to fit us as a family along with our small granddaughter into her office so that my husband could finish adopting my daughter. she even sent me photos of the zoom court conference that I will always treasure, having them to remember our day! I will reach out again with any legal matters I need without hesitation!

We hired Renee to represent us in a parenting plan case and there was a lot involved ex with this case - a lot to digest. We had a special needs child, a 50/50 verbal custody arrangement and a parent who was gone for about seven months and then couch surfing when time for parenting came. That is a very summed up version. Renne was great in person. Easy to talk to, listened, friendly. We had pages and pages of decorations from caregivers, school representative, information on his special needs etc. when it came time for our first time to appear before the judge, Renee was late - she rushed in at the last minute and was chaotically thumbing through papers before we were called up. She started saying things to the judge that were ... Just flat out not true. It was like she was trying to remember things she read but she put so little effort in she was flat out lying (in a way that wasn't even in our favor) to the judge. The judge was berating us for pulling my stepson out of school and moving schools to where we lived (there were multiple reasons we had to do this - his school was an hour away from us where no one loved except his mother who was gone for the next eight months, his school did not have the special needs program to assist and they were sending him home a few times a week because they didn't know what to do with him, etc) and she did nothing to explain or defend this decision while the judge made it out to be a decision to make it easier on us for convenience when that was not the case at all. Afterwards she blamed it on Skagit judges not liking her because she was from Snohomish county. Fast forward she reccomends a guardian ad litem for our case who doesn't respond to any of our emails, calls and who was not ever in office (as in doors locked can't even enter) but she had no problem receiving our retainer check. I can't speak to the other attorneys in this office but Renee was honestly terrible. We spent thousands of dollars to feel like she didn't even read anything we submitted or take any notes or put any effort in. I actually put off leaving a review for a long time but she just sent us a notice withdrawing as our attorney (although she hasn't spoken to us in literal years) which reminded me. Luckily, mom has since gotten her ducks in a row and now everything is going great but it reminded me to share this info.

SUMMARY

I found myself in the unenviable position of needing to hire the services of a family lawyer, and Renee DeFreece at Tuohy Minor Kruse was recommended to me by a friend. I got fantastic service from her and her support team. I would very highly recommend her.

SPECIFICS

My spouse had kicked me out of our house and would only allow me to have four hours per week with our two daughters in a "supervised" setting. This was not, strictly speaking, legal, but I was told it was not actionable without a "parenting plan" in place. Having been an at-home dad for nearly ten years, this was emotionally devastating to me. Renee handled my case with care and respect, and I got the parenting plan I asked for (primary custody of our girls).

RECOMMENDATIONS

I have a graduate-school education, which means I know enough to appreciate Renee's skill. First, she could identify and understand that I was in a lot of emotional pain, and I had only so much "bandwidth" to deal with things. I did not need to know the gory details, just what was relevant. This helped me a lot. Second, I have a little experience with written rhetoric and argumentation, and it is enough for me to see that Renee is very skilled with that. She took the information and documentation I provided her with and was able to identify the best approach. That meant she could edit the legal declaration I wrote to give it as much brevity and impact as possible within the parameters of the court filing, and identify the most important material to use (there are limits on how much you can submit in some cases).

Renee was clear that she was giving me the "driver's seat" if and when I wanted it. What I would recommend to anyone who hires her is to pay very close attention to her recommendations. She will do what you want regardless if she thinks it is in your best interest. I am confident I got the result I wanted precisely because I deferred to her expert advice, and I was humble enough not to think I might know better than her. I was able to see later why she recommended we proceed in certain ways, and it was crystal clear she knew what she was doing all along. I am so very glad I listened to her advice and followed it.

To have my girls restored to me means more than words can express.

CONCLUSION

I can't recommend Renee highly enough. Listen to her expert advice and do it.
I would recommend using them for any matters dealing family law. They were able to help me out a great deal and I was able to win my case. Very smart and organized and professional!

I met Renee a few years back as I started my divorce. From the first minute I knew she was the right lawyer for me. She listened, was caring, compassionate, diligent, knowledgeable and I knew she would be a strong advocate for me. At times when I wanted to give up, she was there to support me and guide me through this difficult process. Quitting is not in her vocabulary and Renee made sure that my outcome was fair and equitable. When I used Renee to set up my Will, I received her great attention to detail to ensure my financial planning was complete. Renee was always there for me with my best interest a priority.

Called on a Wednesday.  No one answered the phone and since i had no idea what attorney to leave a message for the automated option was the general message directory.  It stated someone would return my call promptly.  Two days went by without a call and so I called again on Friday.  This time a woman answered and had no idea I'd left a message.  She told me no one checks that mailbox.  Neat.
Next, I told her why i was seeking counsel and she went through the hourly rates and attorney availability.  Slightly felt like i was bothering her as she scratched out some notes.  She told me that I would receive a call the following Tuesday from Shannon Marsh to see about scheduling a consultation.
I still have yet to receive a call from anyone.
The moral to the story is if you receive poor responsiveness and follow through prior to our first meeting, then the writing is on the wall for the quality of service from this firm.  From the beginning it wreaks of disorganization and lack of professionalism.  I recommend taking your business somewhere else where you feel like your business matters.
Very efficient Dean Minor is very easy to talk too.

Our family is at a 100% success rate with Renee & Stacie, they are a great team. I have been using Renee DeFreece and her team at Tuohy Minor & Kruse for almost five years. She successfully won three different cases for several family members.
When it comes to Family Law, she is fearless and confident.  She is a master at court room professionalism. As far as our family is concerned, Renee is more than a Lawyer, she is an angel. Her Paralegal, Stacie, has been such a blessing and is consistently respectful and efficient.
Money well spent, did not disappoint at all and we got the issue resolved with one phone call

This was the Worst thing I had to go through.. The compassion, professionalism, the knowledge and explanation of Everything, made this as easy as possible!! Renee DeFreese And her Paralegal Stacie are the Absolute Best!! I would 100% Recommend This Law Firm!!!!

I have been using Renee DeFreece and her team at Tuohy Minor & Kruse for almost two years now. My case has been very difficult as it deals with substance abuse by the other parent which we share a 6 year old son. As a father I was worried about not getting proper custody, my son deserves to be properly cared for by a competent parent. Not only has Renee and her team given me the ability to have full control of my son for the last two years but they have been there every step of the way, from day one. They truly care and and are sympathetic to your situation! They work hard and dig into every single angle imaginable, even the ones you don't think of. As a client they have always had my son and my best interest in mind, and when I was unable to see things clearly they helped to guide me in a way that ultimately protected me and my son! They have kept me grounded and set realistic expectations, then not just met the expectations... they have far exceeded them! I have personally recommended Renee and her team to many friends and coworkers in similar situations and will be a client for the duration of not just my case but I will also continue to use them moving forward as long is necessary to get the representation needed so my son will be protected and given the chance to have the best upbringing possible. Two years ago when I was researching family law lawyers, I was referred to Renee by another law firm. I was told she was the best around, her and her team have not failed to impress! As a single dad on a modest income they have worked in a way to get me the highest quality representation all while being creative in ways to keep costs down. Regardless of the cost, they are worth every penny!! It is a huge comfort to know you have someone who truly has my back representing me and my son's interests! They show there passion and drive for looking out for you and not their paycheck! If you are looking for real representation... quit looking, Renee and her team are the real deal!!
I have used this firm several times and Renee is amazing!! Worth every penny for the peace of mind.

Renee was hired as a GAL on our case and refused to bring forth recommendations for the court to order a parental alienation evaluation at the onset of my custody dispute case, with clear and obvious signs of the aforementioned. (Son showing up to visitation with my ex wife’s fiancé’s last name on his clothing, fiance’s name as Dad in my son’s gizmo watch, my son saying fiance was his father, etc...) I went from having my son hold my hand and give me hugs before to swearing at me, being disrespectful and not wanting to be near me all in the matter of 3-4 months and the less time we had together, the worse it got. Crickets from Renee.

After a year of her moving at snails pace and being ill equipped to deal with high conflict custody cases, I paid close to $80k (between 2 lawyers, 2 supervisors, her, time loss from work, [psychiatric evaluation and risk assessment, which came both back normal, no drugs or DV risk] and other miscellaneous costs) to get my son back. I was not successful. DO NOT use her as a GAL. She has no clue how to handle high conflict cases and will drain your bank account through forcing you to use her hand picked, biased professionals, while refusing to recommend the right folks who can potentially end the case much sooner. She acted like it was no big deal and said I could represent myself if costs were getting too high, while working 60-70 hour work weeks to maintain my household and pay all of the open hands of the court system to get my son back. AVOID AT ALL COSTS!
reviews.ownerResponse: Dear Ryan, thank you for sharing your feedback. We set a high standard for ourselves at Tuohy Minor Kruse, and we’re sorry to hear that your experience did not match your expectations. Because of the sensitive nature of our business, we can’t discuss specifics about your case in this forum; however, we are committed to providing the highest quality of services, and we take your feedback very seriously.

reviews.text: Best legal in Snohomish County they are awesome will recommend to everyone!
The Attorneys at Tuohy Minor Kruse go above and beyond for their clients. They're highly referred by peers, a friendly but professional atmosphere.
Worst attorneys ever.

This woman KNOWS what she's doing. I would send ANYONE I cared for to Melanie. If you want someone who is going to work hard, have you back, be there for you.... SHES YOUR GAL. I don't BAT an eye when I have to pay her to take care of me as a client because I KNOW SHE WILL every ..SINGLE..Time.

My experience is long overdue!

I was referred to Melanie's office through a family friend who is a retired lawyer. I had previously hired a lawyer in October of 2016 to help me with my divorce, he did not know what he was doing, he had me fill out unnecessary paperwork and then was supposed to serve my husband, when I asked a week or so later if it happened he said that he couldn't find him and was trying (false), he must have finally submitted the serving papers, because a month later he was served. He would set up meetings often with no reason and nothing come of it. It was like he was lengthening the process and didn't know what he was doing, finally I was fed up with the situation and contacted a few different offices to see if I could get information on costs and process with other ones.

I called Melanie's office and Tina was the one I originally spoke to, she asked me a few questions and provided guidance, I told her I would call her back once I knew what I wanted to do. Tina called me back within 15 minutes and had taken the time to look up my case with the course, apparently I had a meeting in court in 2 days (I was never told this by my lawyer), so I called him and asked if it were true and he said yes he had just received the letter and I had to be there (he had actually received the letter almost a month and a half before). At this point I was bought in and set up a meeting with Melanie and she helped me handle the rest of the process.

I am a person that doesn't like to create more problems in an already problematic situation and avoid confrontation, Melanie was so great with me and listening to my needs! She pushed the other office as they delayed the process and we were finally able to have the whole thing completed within a few months, versus the year or so I had already been waiting with no resolution. She explained all options, was always on my side and didn't push me one way or another, she really put all the choices in my hands as I was going to be the one dealing with it. I could not have been happier with both of them, they are a great team!

Melanie Griffin provided excellent representation in my family law case. She is very professional, always calm and organized. She returns calls and emails promptly, and she was always well prepared. While all attorneys are expensive, this office is professionally managed and everything is very upfront and clearly communicated in terms of charges and billing. In particular, I appreciated Melanie's willingness to collaborate on my case. She was very receptive and willing to listen to my knowledge of the situation and high conflict individual with whom we were dealing. She then took this information and applied her expertise in law to help me navigate a complex and lengthy battle to ultimately obtain a positive outcome. I highly recommend Melanie Griffin at Tuohy Minor Kruse PLLC.

Hired Melanie Griffin at this firm to assist in a divorce with a minor involved.  Gave great guidance planning our strategy and we were successful getting custody.  Would recommend her services to anyone needing a compassionate advocate going through this daunting process.

I hired Melanie for my high conflict and lengthy (12+ months) family law dispute. Throughout our interactions, she was very patient, knowledgeable, reassuring and professional. She listened to my frustrations and was able to alleviate many of my fears for my child. Melanie is an excellent strategist and was able to help arrange the best possible outcome for my case. I highly recommend Melanie's services to anyone needing representation for family law matters in Snohomish County.

The attorneys at this law firm changed my life for the better! I went through a very contentious divorce and with the help of Kara Kruse and Deane Minor and all the staff, I was able to successfully navigate the divorce and a move. I felt they always gave me excellent legal advice and, they were always supportive and helped me stand up for my rights when it came to financial, parenting splits and all the other issues. During such an emotional time, it was wonderful to be able to put my complete trust and faith into someone to look out for and advocate for my best interest. Kara gave me the ability to stand up for myself. She fought tirelessly to help me to get what I deserved, and she helped me keep my children safe and well cared for.  I cannot thank Kara and this firm enough for all of their hard work and support. While I wouldn't wish divorce on anyone, if you have to go through it-use this law firm.

Melanie was my divorce lawyer last year.  I had a lot of concerns about the safety of my children and their futures.  Melanie was there for me throughout the whole process.  She responded quickly and always addressed my concerns. I appreciated her honesty and sincerity that she gave when guiding me through my options, so I was able to make the best choice for my kids and I.  Thank you to the entire staff at Tuohy Minor Kruse, they were always friendly and welcomed me with a smile! I won my case and my children and I haven't been happier!!!!

Choosing Melanie as my divorce attorney was the best decision ever!  She is on top of it, from simple advice to preparing for all of the overwhelming steps of a divorce and custody battle.  It's not going to be fun, but Melanie will guide you through it like a pro.

Melanie Griffin is an excellent attorney. She made me feel at ease from the beginning and made an awful time of my life tolerable. She is quick to respond and will fight for you. She explains legal terms and processes in a way that you can understand them - without making you feel that you lack intelligence. I will continue to recommend Melanie to anyone that I know looking for legal representation.

Compassionate and intelligent representation. More skilled drafters & elocutors do not exist. I had the pleasure of working with Kara Kruse and Melanie Griffin, 2 of the smartest ladies in the biz. They showed up for me in every possible way, kept the wolves at bay & demystified the circus that is litigation. Worth every damn penny.
Absolutely wonderful service, I highly recommend Renee DeFreece. She was very professional, informative, respectful, reliable, trustworthy and efficient.

Over the past few years I've had an on going issue with my former spouse, specifically regarding our parenting plan. Renee, and team were above and beyond helpful in making a stressful time in my life a little more sane.

While I hope that I won't need their services again any time soon,  I will assuredly be calling on them again if I do.

Thanks again Renee!

Melanie Griffin is a great attorney and a wonderful person. When I met with Melanie for the first time regarding my case, she was attentive, understanding, and compassionate. Within days of hiring Melanie, she was helping ease the stress of my case that I had been battling for close to 4 years. Melanie is very knowledge in the law and how to best handle very messy cases. She is a force to be reckoned with in the court room. She is also very well-liked by her colleagues.

Melanie also made me feel like I was her most important client. Melanie would always call me back quickly if she was out of her office. Prompt in responding to my emails and answering every question I had. She would explain to me the process of what she was doing and gave me the choice of how I wanted to handle the situation. She would give me words of support when the case became too much. She always would let me know that she was walking beside me during the whole process.

After 4 years of battling with my ex-husband, thanks to Melanie, I finally have closure and peace in my life. I would never have the outcome I have today if it had not been for Melanie.

All of the staff at Tuohy Minor Kruse is extremely professional and nice. I would highly recommend them (and I have) to anyone.

After accepting a new job in eastern Washington my kids were kidnapped by my ex. I was then accused of beating them as my ex didn’t want me to move. One of the children is not biologically mine and not only was I able to prove the allegations false but won full custody of both children. Melanie and her team fought through all adversity with a non parental custody and a traditional custody battle all the way to the conclusion of the full trial. I won and now live in eastern Washington with full custody of my children. Worth every penny. Thanks to the whole firm but especially Melanie and her paralegal. They worked hard for justice and won. Here’s the kicker I am the father. Men have rights and Melanie protected mine and my kids!

From the time of consultation, until the end of our time together Melanie represented me with an amazing presence. She was quick to respond, informative, honest and very easy to work with. I felt that she strongly understood my concerns and truly wanted to help. I have recommended Melanie & her firm to others seeking legal assistance in the past and will continue to do so in the future!
14k for a 23 year divorce and i really didn't get anything.  Melanie Griffin is a bimbo for a lawyer! I should have gotten a man.

Where to start is the question... This group of women are so FREAKING AMAZING!!! I had been in a custody battle for my daughter for 5 years, I lost the first trial because I did not have a lawyer and at that time they were not allowing non parent custody cases free lawyers and there was no way I could afford one either.

I was FINALLY awarded a lawyer and Blessed to be assigned to Crystal Alford and Associates!
These Ladies are on point ! They fought hard for me and ALWAYS had my back!

I was so proud of Crystal when we finally went to trial! I am starting to cry because Crystal saw all the bull**** I had been put thru for 5 years and the trauma that it had on my daughter and myself.  She knew that I am a Fit parent and fought for me when I thought that no one else would! She gave me my voice back, but most importantly she won my case and now I have my daughter back home with me!

There are no words that can express how GREATFUL I am I have my daughter and she means everything to me!
Just to have someone believe in you when there was no one , not even my entire "family", makes my heart feel full again and gave me the fuel to keep pushing on!

I will always recommend Crystal and her team of angels to any parent that is fighting for their child to go to!

Thank you Crystal, Jessica and Andrea and also the families of these Ladies also because you are their support as well as mine!
Great Law firm! My attorney Britta y Tri has been so helpful and amazing through the process of helping me gain custody of my son! 10/10 for sure! 👌
They are amazing and help you out a lot in your dependant case they are the best lawyers that I've had so far
reviews.ownerResponse: Its been a pleasure working for you.

reviews.text: Crystal and her team are amazing. She truly cares about her clients and works very hard for the best outcome in her cases. I have trusted her twice now with huge life events in my family and I would recommend her to anyone!
Great team who went above and beyond to help me and my kids. Very professional and kind and understanding. Absolutely recommend and a great value for the price.
Crystal hardly responds to emails and in the rare event that she does, it takes over a week and even then the questions are left unanswered.
Hit them up through their website selling help. Unfortunately they never replied or answered my request.
