Going through a difficult point in my life and I found Gregory Davies office through my works EAP. Greg and his team were extremely responsive and helped me through the often confusing legal process. Highly recommended.

I know a lot of lawyers and Mr. Davis is very good lawyer. Good attorney that has been in practice for decades. He has a very good reputation within the court system as being honest and fair, you will never know how important that is until you find yourself in front of a judge; the judge wont know you but he will know the attorney because he has seen him before. Yes I had a case and he listen but was brutally honest which is not what you want to hear; but what you need to hear. That is why I respect him, the courts don't care about the emotion.
Mr. Davies has been our family attorney for a few years. He is very knowledgeable, courteous and helpful. His staff go out of their way to help as well.

My girlfriend was recommended this lawyer for her case in child custody and he failed to complete a open and shut case. It was the simplest case you could have in custody where a man not the father suied for custody and proof was in the dna test. This lawyer had to file several times to get this case taken care of and then failed to file for the countersuite on costs. He was paid for that too but never followed through do not use this lawyer. So not only will he fail to preform his job but he’ll rack up charges that shouldn’t be there just to steal money from his clients that was not rightfully earned. The charges racked up for the cost were not neccessary or authorized on the clients behalf. This review would be zero stars if possible.

My fiance hired Mr. Davies to represent him, against my advice. Mr. Davies acts like he is trying to minimize costs for you. He acts like he cares. He acts like he is on your side. Bottom line though - he in fact does not save you any money. Does not care and is not on your side. He's the "everyone's friend" type and caved the day of court convincing my fiance to settle for worse circumstances then before they walked into the court house, advising my fiance that it was what was best for him (how is worse than before what's best?). Mr. Davies is a great ACTOR! If you are looking for an advocate to fight for your rights, this is not the place.
Very helpful, trues to minimize costs as much as possible.
This lawyer is amazing. He represented me really well.

Laura is a strong, smart, confident and compassionate woman. She took charge and knew just what to do. We let her lead our case without question. She definitely knows the law and fights for her clients rights.

I've been coming to Laura for a couple years with legal questions and occasionally have hired her to help with reviewing documents and writing documents for me on my behalf when I feel I'm completely out of my depth.

I've also hired her back in Oct 2024 (?) after a car accident. Both Laura and her assistant Megan at the time were amazing at handling my case since my insurance company and the other party's insurance company refused to help reimburse me for my medical, can rental, and loss of wages. I had to wait months for repair on my car, and I wouldn't have been able to get a rental car so I could work or compensation for loss of wages without their help.

I respect and trust Laura and her team whole heatedly with any of my legal issues and will be forever grateful for any services they can provide. She's worth her weight in gold!

Hiring Ms Laura Shaver was one best decisions I made for my life. After my arrested, I was facing serious prison time, booked on 6 charges ( 5 of them being serious felonies, 1 being in the 1st degree) Being terrified how this outcome would affect my families well being as the sole provider, I actively was looking for the best possible legal representation. Consultation after consultation I was told how serious these charges were and I’d be lucky to see a plea deal of 3-5 years. I’ve known about Ms Shaver from several cases televised and talked about on the media. I took the chance on reaching out and from the very first interaction she was passionate about working towards giving me the best possible outcome. She would always give it to me straight at the same time, and that’s what I admire most about her. Ms Shaver truly cares about the people she represents and her work shows that. With her in my corner I walked away from my situation with no jail time, was sentenced to 90 days on house arrest and 1 year probation. My family and I are forever grateful to Ms Shaver.
Laura is the absolute best!

Laura is empathetic, caring, and truly has your back. When I was struggling with discrimination and harassment in the workplace, she not only provided excellent legal support but also helped connect me with important resources. Saying she’s your biggest supporter when she’s in your corner is an understatement. Five stars all the way.

A Truly Exceptional Attorney and Human Being

I cannot say enough about the extraordinary dedication and compassion Laura Shaver brought to my case. From the very beginning, I was facing an incredibly stressful and life-altering situation, and Laura not only believed in me—she fought for me. She took the time to understand every detail, personally invested herself in my situation, and treated me like a human being deserving of dignity and fairness.

What impressed me most was how far beyond her role she was willing to go. She helped connect me with additional legal resources and professionals when my case required it, never hesitating to make sure I had the strongest possible support system. It was clear that she wasn’t just doing a job—she genuinely cared about the outcome and about me as a person.

All of this would be remarkable on its own, but the fact that she managed to pour this level of energy, skill, and heart into my defense while also caring for a newborn makes her nothing short of extraordinary. Laura is one of the most resilient, generous, and dedicated people I have ever met.

My life would not be the same without her help. If you are looking for an attorney who is brilliant, compassionate, and truly committed to her clients, Laura Shaver is everything you could hope for and more.

Laura Shaver is not only exceptional in understanding the legal system, she is willing to take each client’s individual case into consideration when deciding how to approach best outcome. Her expertise and willingness to scour for the necessary information and resources makes her highly regarded and trusted. Her open communication and guidance throughout the process builds great trust and confidence in an otherwise difficult situation. Great respect and admiration for Laura Shaver.

A DOC probation officer with a history of misconduct tried to revoke my parole three times. I hired three private attorneys to defend myself, and Laura was the best of them. She wrote a strong, smart defense brief and even used a public records request to force the prosecution to produce a document that helped my case. She thinks creatively and knows the law.

But she was also a diva. She got irritated when I asked for basic updates, even though attorneys are supposed to keep clients informed. She ghosted me before the hearing and again afterward, so I hired someone else.

I’d been burned by my first attorney, so I made it clear I needed to see every brief and exhibit before meeting the judge. When she tried to schedule that meeting without giving me the documents, I requested a continuance. I got it—but then she vanished for three months, held the hearing with minimal communication, and never returned another call or email.

If you don’t need much communication from your attorney, she might work for you. Otherwise, be cautious.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your experience. I’m genuinely glad that, after having worked with multiple attorneys, you found my legal work effective and that the outcome of your hearing was successful. I take that part of your feedback seriously and appreciate the acknowledgment of the advocacy I provided.

I do want to provide some context regarding the communication concerns you raised. The relevant time period coincided with my pregnancy, related medical complications, and subsequent maternity leave, circumstances that were known by you at the time. I returned to work earlier than planned, approximately four weeks postpartum, specifically to handle your hearing so that it could proceed without requiring you to wait several additional months – something also known by you at the time. After the hearing concluded successfully, I resumed my maternity leave, which accounts for the period in which I was unavailable afterward.

I understand that different clients have different expectations regarding communication, and I respect that this may not have been the right fit for you in that regard. That said, my focus was ensuring your hearing was handled effectively and without unnecessary delay, which it was.
I wish you the best going forward and appreciate you taking the time to leave a thoughtful, if mixed, review.

My divorce took roughly 15 months from start to finish. Jack took on my case and was absolutely wonderful. Direct, thorough, no BS. I found him intimidating at times, which came in handy when my ex-husband tried pulling fast ones on me.

Josh ended up taking over a few months later and though he was also fantastic, was incredibly chatty. There were times he would talk for so long I'd forget what we were discussing in the first place. Eventually I complained to him about this, and he took my feedback graciously and adjusted how he communicated.

Overall, I'm very happy with the outcome of my divorce. The team at Berner Law Group are strategic and sharp. I'd recommend them in a heartbeat.

I hired Jack Berner for my high-conflict divorce case after my previous attorney didn’t work out, and it was the best decision I could have made. Before hiring Jack, I spent days reading reviews on various websites trying to choose the right attorney. The reviews were unanimous: if you need a shark, hire Jack. I was fortunate they took my case.

The entire team at Berner Law Group was exceptional. They responded to my inquiries promptly, provided clear and accurate statements, and went above and beyond to support me. My case was headed for trial, but a pre-trial settlement offer was presented at the last minute. Jack and Kirstie worked tirelessly throughout the week and the weekend, preparing for both trial and settlement negotiations.

On the day before trial (a Sunday), Jack spent over 12 hours in his office working on my case. His dedication and persistence were unmatched, and he fought for the best outcome for me until the very last moment.

I’m incredibly grateful to Jack and the entire Berner Law Group team. Their professionalism, hard work, and commitment to their clients are remarkable. I highly recommend them to anyone in need of a strong, reliable legal team.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hafiz! Thank you so much for the great, informative review of our firm (as opposed to the nonsensical, hit & run-type stuff that's seemingly unavoidable in this day and age). I'm very glad you found your way here. Our motto is that we treat your case as if it were our own, and the details you shared with the public reflect that commitment, so thank you for confirming that. As I'd mentioned to you previously, it helps us go above and beyond when we have a great client, and you were certainly a great client. We at Berner Law Group wish you and your family happy holidays and a great New Year!

reviews.text: I contacted Jack to help with a difficult support modification because I knew him to be dedicated to getting the best result possible for clients. What I got instead was the entire Berner Law Group team helping me assemble an effective strategy and comprehensive response for my case. Starting with the initial consult, we talked through details and Jack put me at ease that while it would be difficult, we would handle each point with thoughtfulness. He was honest about best and worst case scenarios which helped prepare me for what would take about 6 months to prepare. The entire team responded quickly due to the initial timeline and clearly communicated immediate needs and longer term items to prepare. Nothing is more stressful for a family than uncertainty and Jack, Josh and team were very reassuring through the process. From start to finish, they were thorough, communicative and responsive. The entire team handled every thing professionally and at a personal level and I was very happy that our case was presented to the judge in a fair and reasonable manner and everything was considered before his final judgment. I would 100% ask Jack and Josh for counsel in the future.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Rob, the fact that you're not only pleased with our services but pleased enough to broadcast it to the general public honors us immensely. Thank you for explaining our processes so well so folks can get an insider's view of how we work. We strive to consistently be realistic with our clients from day 1 (no blowing smoke/bait and switch like the kind commonly found in some other firms' reviews) and then work like hell to get the best possible outcome for our clients. It's reviews like yours--from actual clients who we've gone to battle for--that are the best indicator for would-be clients of what they can expect from our office--namely, strategic thinking, thoughtfulness, honesty, diligence, empathy/reassurance, highly communicative, professional. I couldn't have said it better myself! I hope you never have to use our services again--family law services, anyway--but, if you do, we'd be happy to bring it all over again! Best wishes to you and your family, Rob!

To the public: If you wish to know more about Berner Law Group, PLLC, please contact our office to set up a consult to discuss your own matter. We look forward to hearing from you!

reviews.text: I went to have a consultation and the lawyer there made me almost cry due to his lack of empathy for anyone accused me of being incompetent and was very rude. I do not recommend these people at all told me I was an unmedicated bipolar person.

Jack helped me navigate a difficult situation, and I cannot thank him and his team enough.  Jack made sure that I understood each step of the process, and he was always very upfront and honest with me.  Jack made sure that I was aware of my options, and he supported me in how I chose to proceed.  Thank you Jack!

Although nobody wants to have to involve lawyers in personal matters, sometimes it is necessary as it was in my situation. The staff at BLG work cohesively and thoroughly, with their communication prompt and thorough. Lori has been the primary paralegal that has touched my case, with a few others rotating in. Even with the different paralegals assisting the attorneys, all i's were dotted and t's crossed.

Josh Camp ended up being the attorney assigned to take on my case, and since day 1 he has done an outstanding job. Even with several other cases under his care, his communication has also been prompt and thorough. While being an attorney is his job, it is clear that Josh is also a determined yet caring individual as well - this became evident when I was going through all-day mediation. Josh states the facts and provides guidance, but never drives the decision. I have consistently felt well represented, well informed, and completely supported through this arduous process.

While I wish our paths never had to cross, I am indebted to Berner Law Group for truly being outstanding representing me during the divorce process. I wholeheartedly recommend their law firm if you find yourself in the same circumstances that I did. They are worth every penny!

Their attention to detail, deep understanding of the intricacies of the law, their timeliness and efficiency set them apart from other firms I've encountered. They left no stone unturned in fighting for a favorable outcome, consistently prioritizing my well-being and future security.

I firmly believe that the services provided by the Berner Law Group are worth every penny. In matters as delicate as divorce, the importance of expert guidance and support cannot be overstated. The peace of mind afforded by their expertise and dedication far outweighs any concerns about cost.

All in all, my experience with has been nothing short of exceptional. They have set the standard for professionalism, quality, and integrity in divorce representation. I absolutely recommend their services to anyone seeking compassionate and effective legal counsel during challenging times.

reviews.text:
If I could go back in time with what I know now, I would have hired Berner Law Group in March 2017!  My first attorney was not responsive and too inexperienced to handle this custody case so I decided to look elsewhere. My second attorney who had only a legal assistant to help her took on my complex case. I was dealing with a difficult ex-husband who was trying to alienate my children from me. This case lingered on with a biased, inadequate guardian ad litem (GAL). In July 2019, my second attorney called me up late at night to tell me she couldn't continue with this case as the stress of her legal practice was taking its toll on her health. She had already let go of her legal assistant in prior months, and she was planning to find a new career. She recommended 3 different attorneys stating "if you want a shark, call Jack Berner". I called Jack right away! Thankfully, Jack took on my complex case after giving me the bad news that judges often go with the GAL's recommendations. He promised me that he would do his best to get the best results possible. Jack also said that he would have never allowed this GAL to work with his clients. Jack had a huge challenge ahead but he took on my case with passion, professionalism, and commitment. The amount of emails, Court papers, etc. would make most attorneys’ heads spin but Jack is incredibly intelligent, motivated, and has a great support team that was able to help organize 2+ years of records.

After a long, stress-filled 3+ years custody issue with an unreasonable ex-husband supported by an attorney with a reputation of creating havoc in divorce/custody issues, Jack and his amazing team helped me get exactly what I wanted and was best for my teen daughters! We had a six day Zoom trial this past July 2020, in which, the judge ruled in my favor of what I had requested in mediation: 50/50 shared custody, no child support either way, etc.. After Jack’s thorough cross-examination of the GAL and my ex-husband, the judge threw out every one of the GAL’s recommendations! The case turned out so favorable because of an amazing attorney and his team (Danielle, Lori, and Josh).  Their attention to detail, superior service, and excellent communication is admirable. Jack’s legal assistant Danielle’s upbeat, can-do attitude, quick response, and knowledge, helped me through some very tough times.  Berner Law Group earns an A+ from this teacher! I am forever thankful and feel blessed for what Jack and his team have done for me and my daughters.
reviews.ownerResponse: Kim, it was truly our sincere pleasure! We are beyond humbled by your kinds words and it's truly this kind of feedback that keeps us going day in and day out in this messy line of work that we're in.

reviews.text: I called with urgent questions regarding parenting plan.. I payed the hourly fee for the lawyer to just keep repeating himself with an im not sure... Im so upset. What a waste of money.
reviews.ownerResponse: Unfortunately, I must take serious issue with this “review”.  Given the public anonymity of the poster and lack of any real detail, this appears to me to be more like a “hit piece” which had the intended effect of materially dropping my otherwise exemplary numeric score on Google.  Although the individual does not identify themselves by name, there are enough clues from their other postings (this individual appears to dislike law offices in general?) and responses thereto that I believe I know who this may be.  Based on my notes from said consult and knowing myself in general, the review is materially false (with the exception of the subject matter that they were calling my office about).  First, I do not use the term “I’m not sure”.  If there’s one thing I do not possess a shortage of, it’s opinions.  While I may, on occasion, state that I cannot definitively predict what the Court would do, how could I? I’m not a fortune teller.  I don’t have a crystal ball.  That would depend on a number of factors including both sides’ evidence including the weight of third party intervention (e.g., law enforcement, CPS, etc.), the judicial officer hearing the matter, whether the judicial officer is appealed (e.g., via motion for revision), the caliber of the opposing counsel, the parties’ respective finances to deal with possibly protracted litigation, and numerous other factors—most of which is obviously unknown to me from just an initial phone call.  I have integrity, so I don’t bait and switch, blow smoke, or even sugar coat like many of my competitors routinely do in order to get business in the door, to the later dismay of their clients.  If anything, I tend to under-promise and over-deliver.  If that costs me business, so be it.  The alternative is simply not worth it to me.  I provide recommendations based on my wealth of experience in order to get the best results possible for the individual.  In this particular instance (if the reviewer is who I think it is), my notes reflect that I gave specific, actionable advice—namely, to secure an Immediate Restraining Order from the Court so as not to get in trouble with violating an existing Court Order.  Accordingly, I don’t know if this is a case of shooting the messenger, transference, projection or what.  I also don’t understand how the poster can say I kept repeating myself with “I’m not sure”.  My notes reflect that the consult lasted almost 40 minutes. Accordingly, how is what the poster said even possible time-wise? If I simply repeated what the reviewer attributed to me, I assume that would be the end of the call and that it would have ended within the first 5-10 minutes.  I also don’t know what the poster means when they say they “payed (sic) the hourly fee”.  We bill in 6 minute increments so as to be as fair to the individual as possible. I’m sorry if they are truly upset and/or feel that it was a waste of money.  I did, however, want to set the record straight since they posted their feelings for the general public to see and with the clear intent of trying to harm my business.  What kind of advocate can I be to others if I don’t stand up for myself?  Thankfully, I’m not alone in that respect.  One need only look at the other reviews on here from our dear former/clients including one from just the other day.  They are a true representation of what we endeavor to do here at Berner Law Group for each and every case we take on.  We have been recognized not just by our former/clients but also by numerous ratings institutions for our excellence in the practice of law in Washington State (e.g., Nation’s Top One Percent for 2020 by National Association of Distinguished Counsel, 10 Best Law Firm for Client Satisfaction for 2020 by American Institute of Family Law Attorneys, Attorney and Practice Magazine’s Top 10 Family Law Attorney for 2020, etc.).  All that said, I encourage the poster of the review to contact my office to discuss the matter further if they wish.  We aim to please.

During a time when I was full of fear and anxiety, they provided me concrete details and facts, and made me feel confident that the truth would prevail. This is a stellar law group that deserves all the praise they are given. They helped me and my family through a horrendous guardianship process with expert care and upmost professionalism. I highly recommend this law group because they will get it done and go beyond as needed. I have nothing but gratitude toward this group.

Family Law is a daunting field to contemplate from a disinterested perspective, and the vast array of counter-intuitive and complex rules and standards of procedure quickly turn to the terrifying for the unfortunate citizen who is personally involved in their outcome. It was in this unenviable position that I found myself not long ago, and I can frankly aver that the first call I made to Berner Law Group marked the most fortuitous turning point in my case that I could have wished for.

I cannot stress enough that while standards for admittance to the bar in Washington may well be consistent, by no means is every family law attorney equal in skill or dedication. Without particular disparagement to any one firm or practitioner, let me say with utmost confidence that of the many I have interacted with thus far, Jack Berner, Josh Camp, and their dedicated staff have proven the most professional, attentive to detail, and outright effective of any, without exception or qualification.

The subject of expense is frankly unavoidable when discussing law firms or contemplating their retention. Let me leave it by saying that while this case was by no means inexpensive for me, I have found Berner Law Group's rates entirely fair given the amount and quality of work on their plate, and that while the adage that 'one gets what one pays for' holds truer in litigation than anywhere else, I have also seen less satisfactory work from more expensive attorneys.

In short, I have come away from my case with the utmost gratitude and respect for this firm, the work they do and the dedicated staff which undertakes it. I would not hesitate to recommend them to any party whose legal success I wished to see realized, and they will remain my first choice for any subsequent representation or advice I should require in future.

reviews.text: Mr. Berner tells it like it is. Being direct and competitive is something that I admire in an attorney and Jack knows what it takes to win. It's easy to get caught up in emotions when talking about a legal matter but his "get down to business" mentality helps redirect your focus on advancing your position rather than wasting time on other things. When you pay as much as you do in lawyer fees, his respect for your time and resources is as professional as it gets. I'll be asking for him the next time I need a lawyer who fights for what's in my best interest
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Toby!  I truly appreciate the review you left about us and am making a point of responding to all such reviewers who went out of their way to talk about their experience with our office, even if a while back.  I am pleased that you're a fan of the "get down to business" approach--or "no b.s." approach, as we like to call it.  You are correct that we're not interested in wasting people's time or their money.  For example, we gave hard truth to 4 different potential clients so far this week on issues ranging from child support modification to parenting plan modification to relocation to stepparent adoption.  These are cases where many--if not most--other attorneys would have eagerly taken them at their significant hourly rates, and burned through $20K+ with probably nothing to show for it.  We see those results from others weekly while attending Court hearings.  We're not the firm that will "give it the ole' college try" and call it good.  If we think you have an uphill battle, we'll flatly tell you so that you can make as informed a decision as possible as to whether to file the action at all and, if so, what the best strategy would be going forward. No bait and switch, no smoke blown, no b.s.

reviews.ownerResponse: From the attorney of record: “I take any client concern or complaint very seriously and could provide some important explanatory context and background regarding this particular complaint, but I am bound professionally to maintain confidentiality regarding all of my and my firm’s clients – even when a negative review like this is posted.  I am certainly sorry for this client’s dissatisfaction but, unfortunately, my advice to some clients and my assessment of some clients’ issues will not be what the client wants to hear or was hoping for.  Nevertheless, even when I know that the client will be disappointed, I will always endeavor to provide that client with honest, objective professional advice and to bill fairly for my services. GEICO gaslighted me and hadn’t wanted to pay for all the tort and damage.

Sean said, GEICO practiced time delayed responses and low ball offers. Sean also said GEICO should be careful because what they were doing could have easily cost them a second, completely separate claim against themselves called, “Bad Faith,” if they kept refusing to come up on their offers..

The point is, Dubin Law Group completely understands Personal Injury Law. They are not afraid to put on their suits and armor and defend their clients to the end.

Dubin Law Group is truly a genuine A+++ Law “firm.”

By the way, thanks for the Christmas blanket. You all didn’t have to. And it was a completely unexpected gift that made me smile when I found it by random on my front porch. It’s very warm and cozy. 🥰

Melissa Sullivan recently assisted me with a traffic infraction that occurred in a remote area where traveling to contest the ticket would have required missing work. While the infraction itself was not excessively costly, I was concerned about the impact it could have on my driving record and insurance rates.

From the outset, Mellissa was professional, clear, and thorough in explaining her approach, what information she needed from me, the potential outcomes, and the likelihood of any court appearance. She communicated expectations upfront and made it clear that her primary goal was to seek dismissal of the infraction.

Throughout the process, Melissa kept me informed at every step, and she ultimately succeeded in having the infraction dismissed without requiring any additional expense or a court appearance on my part. I am very grateful for her knowledge, experience, and effective representation. I highly recommend Mellissa Sullivan to anyone seeking reliable and professional legal representation.

My case: DUI.  With complications.  High BAC.  Collision involved.  Open and shut case against me.

There were several law firms I could have hired.  Nothing bad at all to say about the other firms I spoke with.  Melissa stood out head and shoulders above the other firms I spoke with based on her overall experience, but especially her prosecutorial experience.  Her knowledge and skill, plus her ability to communicate, stood out when we spoke.

Outstanding service and professionalism!  As events unfolded, Melissa ALWAYS kept me promptly informed of what to expect, next steps, and timing for me to complete actions.  She is always super-prepared, and if you follow her advice things will go super-smoothly.

Melissa also is fantastic to work with! Totally accommodating of my work schedule and when it was best for me to take calls. She explained everything and put me at ease right up front. In my situation, I have vocational factors with regulatory impacts outside of my DUI case.  Melissa was extra-patient with me discussing these outside considerations while we worked my case. Fortunately she also has a great sense of humor!

Finally, and perhaps most importantly, Melissa delivers RESULTS.  She took care of everything so I didn't have to worry.  She negotiated positively and constructively with the prosecutor to reduce my charge to reckless driving with the absolute minimum sentence.

This was not an easy case.  It would've been a slam/dunk against me but Melissa achieved an infinitely better outcome than I could've imagined.  THANK YOU MELISSA!

Don't drink and drive.  Everybody says that.
Though trust me, you will not want to be staring down those consequences.

...But if you ever need somebody in your corner, DEFINITELY HIRE MELISSA!

Paul was the only lawyer who was willing to take my case for something I could actually afford, and put in more work than I could possibly have asked of any lawyer at any price. He is honest, humble, funny, as well as well-versed and tireless in his pursuit of justice. No one wants to need these services, but if you find yourself in need of these services you want a  human who knows the law from both a prosecution and defense perspective, is well versed in the law and systemic issue, and genuinely cares about his clients. Melissa is such a joy to work with she is incredible and fights for her clients. I was very lucky to have her on my side and I am so grateful for all her help. Also incredibly fairly priced!

I can't thank her or recommend her enough.  If you've gotten yourself in a pickle, call her NOW, she is the real deal. Thank you so much again, Melissa, I'd be in a world of trouble without you. Hiring Melissa Sullivan from Sullivan Law Group was the best decision I made for my legal representation for a DUI.  My journey began on October 31st, 2020 when I made a terrible choice to drive my car to go get food while intoxicated. I was pulled over entering my apartment complex as I parked at my assigned spot.  I knew what happened.  I knew I was wrong.  I cooperated with the officer.  I ended up blowing 3x the legal limit of .08.  I sat in jail for 20 hours.

Shortly thereafter, I searched for a DUI attorney.  Melissa was the first I contacted.  I felt immediate ease after explaining what happened.  I felt comfortable listening to her.  Then, I consulted with a second attorney who did not quite put me at ease. I decided Melissa was the clear choice. Right away, Melissa guided me on the right track.  She advised me in great detail on what exactly I needed to do.  I followed up immediately.  I attended and completed the classes along with the required assessment.  Also, I paid to appeal my license suspension with the DOL.  While doing this, Melissa got me a continuance since she knew I'd be on vacation out of town during the Christmas/New Year holidays.

The big news came when Melissa informed me the prosecutor agreed to a plea deal of negligent driving in the 1st degree, which was the desired goal!  Next, we found out the DOL would be dismissing our case!  Melissa subpoenaed the officer to appear at the DOL hearing however he no-showed.  Our chance of winning was around 20%.  We won.

Before my final court appearance, Melissa went over everything the judge would ask.  She put me at ease.  Even though the judge issued slightly higher fines, I was not ordered anything beyond the agreed plea deal.

In conclusion, while Melissa apologized to me for not getting lower fines, she absolutely "Hit It Out Of The Park".  I'm grateful for Melissa Sullivan's guidance throughout my legal journey.  I'm most grateful I did not cause any harm to others from my action. I would highly recommend Melissa Sullivan at the Sullivan Law Group to anyone who needs a DUI attorney.  Top notch professional service.

Thank You Melissa!

Nate

Karen Halverson defended me in a parole matter, and while she was willing to meet in person, the rest of the experience was disappointing and unnecessarily expensive.
Billing:
She charges $425/hour, but billed that rate for basic administrative tasks a secretary should handle—like an $85 charge just to reply to an email about billing. She also charged a full hour for an initial consultation I never agreed would be billable, before I had even decided to hire her. In the end, she cost twice as much as two other attorneys who do the same type of work.
Preparation:
I didn’t receive her written brief until the day of the hearing, and was never shown the exhibits beforehand. Because of this, she missed a key defense argument I could have given her if she had shared the evidence.
Afterward:
Once the hearing was over, she basically disappeared. It took over a year—and a complaint to the bar association—to get the leftover retainer back.
Overall Impression:
Karen is clearly skilled in the law, but the lack of preparation and overbilling weren’t worth it. If she now has administrative support, use her if you can afford it. If she’s still flying solo, I’d steer clear.
Great Defense Attorney

Snohomish County prosecutors effectively put my daughter and I in danger due to a clerical error on their end. All I received was an “oh well!” email as they swept us under the rug and moved on with their lives. I hate the Washington Legal System.

These people are the biggest hindrance to justice in Snohomosh County. They don't understand probable cause or all of the laws in the RCW. They will refuse to prosecute property crimes outright and won't prosecute if they aren't familiar with that law. They also refuse to prosecute a crime if the warrant was not reviewed by their office prior to going to a judge. Numerous felony cases are declined because they failed to charge them within the statute of limitations (3 years).  They look for every opportunity to drop a case and care nothing for the safety of the residents of Snohomish County. They are an embarrassment to the justice system.
They accepted the lie the accused gave the court without question and dropped the case. I'm surprised anyone is convicted in Snohomish county.

Last June, my roommate (at the time) nearly set my alarm on fire. She had 4 children in her care at the time, her two and my two. I was under the impression that she was fine with the children, which she told me and a neighbor. Two hours after I got to work that morning, I got a call from the Everett Fire Department. This woman (I'll respectively leave out her name) had attempted to cook all four children Top Ramen for breakfast and passed out drunk. ALL four children tried waking her up. It took my two older girls unlocking and opening windows and screaming for a neighbor to help. My oldest daughter let in one of our neighbors and it took an ambulance and SNIFFING SALT to wake her up. I had to leave work after 2 hours to get my children. I lost my job that day, I wasn't allowed to walk into MY home until I promised not to harm this person who put your innocent lives at danger. My children had to watch their best friends at the time get taken into protective custody. I was told by the Snohomish County Sheriff's Department that I couldn't press charges, and that the state wouldn't press charges. I was also told that since this woman (because of her kids) had been staying with me for more than 10 days, I couldn't even kick her out.

Does ANY of this sound right to ANYONE?!

reviews.ownerResponse:
Attorney-client privilege prevents me from responding to specific comments about any case.
In every matter, we carefully discuss the risks, benefits, and available options. Clients have the constitutional right to proceed to trial or to resolve a case by plea. My role is to provide informed advice based on experience and the circumstances presented.
The final decision always belongs to the client. These decisions are significant and personal, and while most clients feel confident in their choice, some later have mixed feelings. I remain committed to providing thoughtful guidance so each client can make the decision that is right for them.
- Attorney Scott Lawrence

Philip didn't know that I even paid my trial fee up front and it was never refunded even though we got no where close to trial. This really was my worst experience for anyone I have ever hired. I feel he fought harder with me than the prosecution.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Michel,
Thank you for your feedback, however I find your review to be unfair and incorrect. Our office worked diligently on your case and achieved an great result - charges dismissed!
Your complaint regarding unrefunded trial fees would be understandable, except that the fee you paid was a trial preparation fee. This is one of the things we discussed while going over the contract, along with your responsibility to pay for investigator fees.
One of the benefits of retaining our office is that we have two attorneys that are able to represent you, however with the current pandemic it is difficult to meet face to face and I apologize if you did not recognize your representation during virtual hearings.
Snohomish Law Group aims to provide great service and achieve a great result for our clients and I apologize if we fell short in your eyes.
We wish you luck in your future endeavors.

This was our first time ever needing a lawyer, and it was for a very emotional and stressful custody battle. While we won’t share the details of our case or the outcome, we feel compelled to speak to our experience working with Jennifer Bitner and her paralegal, Shannon.

When we first came to Jennifer, we were overwhelmed—scared, frustrated, angry, confused, and unsure of where to even begin. From that very first conversation, Jennifer immediately made us feel assured. Her transparency, knowledge, and straightforward approach gave us a sense of empowerment we didn’t know we needed. She was honest in setting our expectations, never giving us false hope, but her confidence, preparation, and strategy made us feel like we had the best possible person in our corner.

Jennifer’s experience and dedication, along with Shannon’s incredible support, were exactly what we needed during such a difficult time. Shannon was not only a source of emotional support but also a fierce and dependable part of the team. Together, they formed a powerhouse duo that we trusted completely.

Thanks to their hard work, we achieved the result we came to Jennifer for—and more. We would not hesitate to recommend Jennifer and Shannon to anyone facing the challenges of family court. Their professionalism, expertise, and compassion are unmatched, and they made one of the most stressful experiences of our lives manageable. Should we ever need legal assistance again, we would return to them without question. If you’re navigating the difficult road of a custody case, you could not ask for a better team to guide you.

Thank you so much Jennifer and Shannon, you really help get us through me of the hardest times of our lives. Thank you!!!

Jasmine’s calm and empathetic demeanor offered me so much needed emotional support during a time that the likelihood of a satisfactory resolution seemed impossible. She always displayed a high level of professionalism, even when things got heated with opposing counsel. She exemplified honesty and kindness. She always stepped forward with the truth and held strong
that it would prevail.

After a long 3-day trial (not to mention months of legal wrangling and negotiation), Jasmine prevailed on my behalf and succeeded in winning even more than I had originally requested. I
highly recommend Jasmine Mitchell without any reservation. She is the ally you want and the advocate you need by your side during the most trying times in your life.

From the very first consult Damon was understanding and knowledgeable, provided me with information I didn't even know I needed, and assured me that I wasn't alone or overreacting about the issues that prompted me to start legal action to begin with. Hannah also fiercely represents me in court, and makes sure I understand my own action items and what steps come next or what to expect. Damon and Hannah have been my voice during a very challenging time, and I am happy to have them on my team.

Jennifer has also become a friendly face to see, and is always happy to jump in where needed and provide information that helps me move forward. I worked with Shannon briefly as well, which was also a pleasant and informative experience.

Patty is an absolute delight, and I love our chats whenever I come in way too early. Which is every time.

Canfield Madow clearly works as a team, and while I hope I won't need them very often, I know whenever I do they will be there and they will be the first people I call. Or, more realistically, email.

Damon’s conference calls with me regarding assets and possible  divorce were extremely helpful in calming me down, which in turn has allowed me to make better decisions for this situation.
